name: Holcomb Crew
subject: ICMYI: Eric just gave the keynote speech at the 2020 Indiana Republican State Convention
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
He couldn’t share his vision for the next four years in front of 2,000 other Hoosier Republicans due to social distancing, but he was still ecstatic.. | | |
---|---|---
Friends,

**Eric just gave the keynote speech at the 2020 Indiana Republican State Convention.**

Because of COVID-19, he couldn’t share his vision for the next four years in front of 1,800 other Hoosier Republicans, but he was still ecstatic to talk about his plans for Indiana’s future.

In case you weren’t able to tune in, our team pulled together some of the best moments from his speech below.
**Eric on Indiana’s Resilience... **
_“This year, we’ve been tested in unimaginable ways. A pandemic cut short the lives of too many Hoosiers and brought our roaring economy to a sudden halt. And an act of abhorrent brutality in Minnesota ended George Floyd’s life and brought into national focus a needed reckoning on matters of racial equality. Through it all, we’ve shown the world what a resilient, welcoming, and big-hearted place Indiana is... and that’s why we’ve been able to make some progress... and are on track to continue to make some more.”_
|
---
**Eric on the progress we’ve made so far...**
“Lowering infant mortality, fighting the drug epidemic, investing in our urban centers, expanding education and job training opportunities... these actions are having real results. But we’re far from done... in terms of healthcare, criminal justice reform, affordable housing, economic development in our inner cities, as well as policing standards and race relations for those disproportionately affected. But we Hoosiers have never been afraid of hard work... in fact we relish a challenge.”
|
---
**Eric on where we’re going in the next four years...**
_“Becoming One Indiana For All means getting Hoosiers back to work and connecting more with higher-wage jobs. It means attracting more business leaders, job creators and entrepreneurs to Indiana. One Indiana For All means working together to overcome racial inequality._

_It means taking a long look at some of the restrictions and regulations we set aside during the pandemic to see which ones can go away permanently. One Indiana For All means continuing to modernize your state government... making sure it is running efficiently for the taxpayers... and shrinking those elements that are barriers to progress. It means finishing I-69. It means taking long-lingering health issues—head on. It means hitting our goal of being the best state in the Midwest for infant mortality by 2024. We’re on our way, but we’re not done yet.”_
|
---
**Eric on what he’s asking from Hoosiers...**
_“So... I’m asking you to join us on this mission._

_Because if we want to recover quickly... If we want to tackle issues of social equality that have festered for too long... If we want to get our state at full employment again... If we want a smaller, more efficient government, better equipped schools, better roads, better technology and better health..._

_If we want to not only GROW the highest quality corn, livestock, and timber but also GROW OPPORTUNITIES for more Hoosiers to climb life’s ladder of success... and rise as high as they choose to go... If we're to MAKE not only world-class autos, RV’s and medical devices, but also MAKE INVESTMENTS cultivating our pro-growth ecosystem so our creative class and our universities of higher learning continue to churn out the discoveries of tomorrow..._

_If we want to accomplish all these things, we can’t... “turn back the clock”._

_As Coach Dale says in Hoosiers our “team’s on the court.”_

_My friends... I’m asking you to do everything you can to make sure we stay there. I’m humbled by your support... honored to serve as your governor... and ready to keep the pedal to the medal for four more years..._

_**Because the best is yet to come.”**_
We’ve been through a lot in the last couple of months, but we’re better prepared than ever to take on the challenges of the future and build One Indiana thanks to Eric’s leadership.

Thank you for reading and we hope that you are as inspired by Eric’s vision for the future as we are.

If you want to check out the full speech, you can watch at **[this link.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=cac85edd93d842599a383256f0bd6b75&_e=FsL5Dc-IQFWmrp1DKNiarpckiuytHi_OyRmYzP89hiIEvO5glNcyyek5c9nIFcPDfGk0U_tflSoZnARcVrLom9spkjUyyaNYd-aN8wcPzquVxZP1aCS3PWw1aqhRXkEca2yd3aDtMTVTayFIXb6MzN4tfaYWn2aoMOLI8ovZn6sAFBOtutYZzKguI8b2s-fKGJzouAQJt1gjs45Rj66BLgpC6z9WHT3n9cO9KcN4qmoYLLVhbas1l3b04l9ls3BFvsxjz6Si7qfhhI-1I_kx-d-4yarQYVlyRbOVmfThNOFpNap0Q5g0jcKHucaEb4RZr-kix3ptdtyOjz9BszBmOUfxit_UOiAxmfC42Vxb2Fs3buEH857vWEHdTVLA6HhyBmT6TAvCYhzxqPQ7xO0VNVETFR1gSljWztOesDer44a2KFaPHyS1eOL7s9WJ8Lww) **

Thank you for your support,

Your Holcomb Crew

name: Dr. Manny Sethi
subject: My opponent is lying about me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Manny for Senate, All rights reserved
body:
[](https://mannysethi.cmail19.com/t/r-l-jkjhllkk-uunkhiuw-y/)
---
| |
---|---|---
| Friends, I wish I could say I can't believe it, but yesterday we saw the same old Washington political tricks... I'll be blunt - my opponent lied. Writing in the Jackson Sun, Bill Hagerty said "my opponent has received the endorsements of NARAL and Planned Parenthood." This is a flat-out lie. I have never been endorsed by any abortion organization. I am pro-life. Period. As a doctor, I know that life begins at conception. And even worse, he knows this. This is what we are fighting against - a career politician who spouts complete lies about his opponent. I need your help to make sure he does not represent honest Tennesseans in Washington.
##### Would you consider helping us fight back against this type of dishonest campaigning?
[Donate $25](https://mannysethi.cmail19.com/t/r-l-jkjhllkk-uunkhiuw-j/) Donate $50 [YOU CAN COUNT ON ME](https://mannysethi.cmail19.com/t/r-l-jkjhllkk-uunkhiuw-t/) Tennessee can't afford dishonest campaigning and more dishonest politicians, that's why I'm running. I'm a physician, not a politician. See you soon, Dr. Manny Copyright © 2020 Paid for by Dr. Manny for Senate, All rights reserved.

clean_body: | | | | ---|--- | | = It's official, Peter. The Cook Political Report moved the Indiana Attorney General's race from "leans Republican" to toss-up! They know what we're seeing on the ground: Hoosier Democrats have the momentum to win this seat. = The stakes couldn't be higher. Attorney General Curtis Hill is suing to take 400,000 Hoosiers' health care away in the middle of a global pandemic. The Republican AG playbook couldn't be clearer: put quality affordable health care out of reach for Hoosier families. We can't give Hill, Todd Rokita, or another Republican another four years to dismantle the progress we've made on health care. = 2020 is the year we vote out Curtis Hill, protect health care for hundreds of thousands of Hoosiers and flip the AG from red to blue. But we can't do that with your help. = Every dollar goes toward making calls, running digital ads, and ensuring Hoosier Democrats cast ballots in November so we can take back the AG's office. name: Melissa Hortman
subject: The world is watching
disclaimer_text: paid for by the DFL House Caucus
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/k-pmzdoM0jyzuYHgXzQ4YNJNeKq982Uz01l4v36re4Y=/b52utbNx_PWZ4JbOEuNht40YIdufRee064txT8lqoQE=?refcode=061920)
| | Friend, In the middle of a global pandemic, a special session of a divided legislature, and nation-wide civil unrest against systemic racism, the need to hold and expand the House DFL majority has never been more important. The People of Color and Indigenous Caucus in the Minnesota Legislature is diligently leading the way on public safety reform. Last night, we voted on the Minnesota Police Accountability Act in order to make the systemic change that will lead to accountability. The world is watching, but Senate Republicans have said that today they'll pack up and go home instead of working to pass meaningful reforms with us. We need to protect and expand our DFL majority in the Minnesota House so that next session, hopefully with a DFL-controlled Senate, we can continue the work of reimagining and reforming how public safety works. Will you make an investment in our campaign to make that a reality? _If you 've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately_ [**Donate $25**](https://act.myngp.com/el/k-pmzdoM0jyzuYHgXzQ4YNJNeKq982Uz01l4v36re4Y=/b52utbNx_PWZ4JbOEuNht40YIdufRee064txT8lqoQE=?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=061920) [**Donate $50**]( https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hdfl-jun2020?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=061920) [**Donate $75**]( https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hdfl-jun2020?express_lane=true&amount=75&refcode=061920) [**Donate $100**](https://act.myngp.com/el/k-pmzdoM0jyzuYHgXzQ4YNJNeKq982Uz01l4v36re4Y=/b52utbNx_PWZ4JbOEuNht40YIdufRee064txT8lqoQE=?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=061920) [**Other Amount**](https://act.myngp.com/el/k-pmzdoM0jyzuYHgXzQ4YNJNeKq982Uz01l4v36re4Y=/b52utbNx_PWZ4JbOEuNht40YIdufRee064txT8lqoQE=?express_lane_other=true&refcode=061920) _You can donate $50 (or $100 per couple) and it will be refunded to you by the state through the Minnesota Political Contribution Refund Program._ I know that together, we can build a better future for all Minnesotans, no exceptions. Thank you, **Melissa Hortman** Speaker Minnesota House of Representatives [**_P.S. Each year you can donate $50 (or $100 per couple) to a campaign and it will be refunded to you by the state. Act Now._**](https://act.myngp.com/el/k-pmzdoM0jyzuYHgXzQ4YNJNeKq982Uz01l4v36re4Y=/b52utbNx_PWZ4JbOEuNht40YIdufRee064txT8lqoQE=?refcode=061920) [**Donate**](https://act.myngp.com/el/k-pmzdoM0jyzuYHgXzQ4YNJNeKq982Uz01l4v36re4Y=/b52utbNx_PWZ4JbOEuNht40YIdufRee064txT8lqoQE=?refcode=061920)

clean_body: | | Friend, In the middle of a global pandemic, a special session of a divided legislature, and nation-wide civil unrest against systemic racism, the need to hold and expand the House DFL majority has never been more important. The People of Color and Indigenous Caucus in the Minnesota Legislature is diligently leading the way on public safety reform. Last night, we voted on the Minnesota Police Accountability Act in order to make the systemic change that will lead to accountability. The world is watching, but Senate Republicans have said that today they'll pack up and go home instead of working to pass meaningful reforms with us. We need to protect and expand our DFL majority in the Minnesota House so that next session, hopefully with a DFL-controlled Senate, we can continue the work of reimagining and reforming how public safety works. Will you make an investment in our campaign to make that a reality? If you 've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately You can donate $50 (or $100 per couple) and it will be refunded to you by the state through the Minnesota Political Contribution Refund Program. I know that together, we can build a better future for all Minnesotans, no exceptions. Thank you, Melissa Hortman Speaker Minnesota House of Representatives | Prepared and paid for by the DFL House Caucus. name: Emily Cain
subject: Looking ahead
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY MARGARET GOOD FOR CONGRESS
body:
As executive director of EMILY’s List, one of my favorite parts of my job is working with the brave women who have stepped up to run for office in every corner of the country. Election Day will be here before we know it, and I’m thrilled to let you know why I support Margaret Good.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17931529/211351246/-940294256?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODIwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NjQ0ODQxZC0zOGIyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=chvoIZmlqbaPjCqBIer88eVKxHkFh4NVvhbdpTOilnc=&emci=1c98efaa-a2b1-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=5644841d-38b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=9814644) Hi there,

As executive director of EMILY’s List, one of my favorite parts of my job is working with the brave women who have stepped up to run for office in every corner of the country. Election Day will be here before we know it, and I’m thrilled to let you know why I support Margaret Good.

I know that when she gets to Washington, she’ll protect the right to choose, expand affordable and accessible health care, and fight for Suncoast communities.

**[Margaret’s facing a critical end-of-quarter deadline at the end of the month, and what she is able to raise will determine the course of the campaign. Please, make a contribution to her campaign today.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17931530/211351247/1073285021?refcode=em06192020&amount=25&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODIwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NjQ0ODQxZC0zOGIyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=chvoIZmlqbaPjCqBIer88eVKxHkFh4NVvhbdpTOilnc=)**

I don’t have to tell you how much is at stake this year, but with the help of committed supporters like you, friend, we can expand the House majority and send more leaders and fighters like Margaret to Washington.

At EMILY’s List, we know that early money can make the difference in what a campaign is able to accomplish and how many voters they can reach.

**[Here’s what that means for you: don’t wait! Make a contribution now to ensure Margaret’s campaign is able to lay the groundwork for success in November.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17931531/211351248/1073285021?refcode=em06192020&amount=25&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODIwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NjQ0ODQxZC0zOGIyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=chvoIZmlqbaPjCqBIer88eVKxHkFh4NVvhbdpTOilnc=)**

Your support today means we can flip one more seat and build on the progress we made in 2018. Your support means Margaret will have the resources to win in November and bring progress to Florida’s 16th District!

Thanks — and go team!

Emily Cain
Executive Director
EMILY’s List
| [Donate »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17931532/211351249/1073285021?refcode=em06192020&amount=25&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODIwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NjQ0ODQxZC0zOGIyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=chvoIZmlqbaPjCqBIer88eVKxHkFh4NVvhbdpTOilnc=)

| Margaret Good is a lawyer, runner, and state representative running in FL-16 to change the face of Southwest Florida, and we are so proud to have you on our team. Margaret is a strong voice for our values—she brings an incredible work ethic and common sense to her fight for working families, to protect Florida’s fragile environment, for accessible and affordable healthcare, and against the special interests that have hijacked our political process. _We need to send her to Congress to continue fighting for us._

Email is the best way for us to keep you informed about what’s happening with the campaign, and we are excited to have your support! body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17933601/211388093/-276570889?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83NDEzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNWYwZjZmNy00NWIyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Vz1uJDw7QzKTM33_amYT5pypeY62CZDSjUcGQLJppR4=&emci=bc95dbfb-28b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=05f0f6f7-45b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=10438639) _Rick Kennedy (D-Central Texas) is running for the open seat in the 17th District.[**Chip in to his campaign here >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17933603/211388095/1282705599?refcode=06192020_em_su_tk_b1_all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83NDEzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNWYwZjZmNy00NWIyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Vz1uJDw7QzKTM33_amYT5pypeY62CZDSjUcGQLJppR4=)_
* * *
---
Dear Friend, I wish you all very happy, solemn, and reflective Juneteenth. __This__ Juneteenth falls during a significant moment, when perhaps America is finally ready to confront the police brutality, institutional racism, and structural disadvantages that have beset the African American community for decades. On this day of celebration for the African American community, I commit that I will continue to listen, learn, and stand with you as your ally.
Thank you, and stay safe. Rick Kennedy
| Paid for by Rick Kennedy for Congress

Rick Kennedy | Paid for by Rick Kennedy for Congress Rick Kennedy for Congress 2203 Emmett Pkwy Austin, TX 78726 To unsubscribe, please .

name: MJ Hegar
subject: Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
We have much more work to do.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648901b7/7f51f7bc/4eac1dea/4ef6db38/656662457/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | Peter,

On Juneteenth, we mark the emancipation of enslaved people in our country and recommit to the fight for racial justice and social equity.

Black Americans have endured generations of pain and injustice -- from slavery, to Jim Crow and segregation, to a criminal justice system that disproportionately harms and kills them. We can't let it continue.

Texas is the site of this turning point in our country's history and I don't take that lightly. It highlights an ugly history in our state, and it also shows that Texas can be a place where transformative change happens.

155 years later, _how_ we choose to continue the fight for true equality will define who we are. For far too long the work has fallen on those most directly harmed by our country's injustices -- especially Black Americans. That's not right. We must _all_ step up, pledge to do the hard work, and keep pushing until we truly achieve liberty and justice for all.

We've got a lot of work to do, and we just can't get it done with a senator who repeatedly denies the reality of systemic racism in our criminal justice system. **[We need real and sustained systemic fixes for what is without question a systemic problem.](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648901b7/7f51f7bc/4eac1dea/4ef6db3f/656662457/VEsE/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://mjfortexas.com/june1email/")**

The work doesn't stop at policing and criminal justice reform, though. All Texans deserve fairness and equity in housing, education, our health care system, and the economy. I'm fighting for a Texas that is at the forefront of these changes, a leader in the fight against racial injustice in our country.

Texas has been and can still be a place where transformative change happens. Let's celebrate today and then wake up tomorrow to continue the work -- together.

Thank you for everything you do to help our nation live up to its ideals.

MJ

P.S. I know that my perspective is limited, and I don't have all the answers. If you would like to share your perspective or have any feedback for me, **[please send in your thoughts here.](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648901b7/7f51f7bc/4eac1dea/4ef6db3e/656662457/VEsF/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/s/mj-CJR")**
---

|
---

| It's clear we need a real leader for Texas and a Senate that prioritizes our health care. **[More than ever, we're relying on online fundraising to keep building the campaign we need to win in November, and we greatly appreciate your continued support>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648901b7/7f51f7bc/4eac1dea/4ef6db3d/656662457/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE1NzIifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-covidfooter?refcode=1572&amount=10")**

However, we know this is not an easy time for many. If you'd like to pause receiving fundraising emails for the next few weeks, **[please let us know here](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648901b7/7f51f7bc/4eac1dea/4ef6db3c/656662457/VEsD/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/s/mj-covid-fewer-emails")**. We'll still keep in touch with important information and to ask your thoughts from time to time.
---

MJ Hegar was a member of the US Air Force and the Air National Guard. Use of her military rank, job, titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement from the Department of Defense, Air Force, or Air National Guard.

MJ for Texas
P.O. Box 7156
Round Rock, TX 78683

Contributions or gifts to MJ for Texas are not tax-deductible.

| | Paid for by MJ for Texas

name: =?utf-8?Q?Dani=20Brzozowski?=
subject: Celebrate Pride with Charlotte Clymer and Sharice Davids!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dani for Illinois
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/0467e34dffc4/celebrate-pride-with-charlotte-clymer-and-sharice-davids?e=140a9b81e7)
---
| |
---
| |
#
I am excited to invite you to join**special guests Charlotte Clymer and US Rep. Sharice Davids at a Virtual Pride Celebration on Jun 27th!**

Charlotte Clymer is an LGBTQ advocate, writer, and former service member whose work has put her own identity at the center of her activism. She is courageous and smart and I am thrilled to have this opportunity to work with her.

Sharice Davids - along with Deb Haaland - is one of the first two Indigenous women to serve in Congress and the first openly gay Indigenous woman in this role. She ran in a tough district and floored so many people when she won in 2018. Her district is home for much of my family and I am filled with admiration for this woman.

---
| [Grab your tickets to join Charlotte and Sharice!](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=29e107d5f4&e=140a9b81e7 "Grab your tickets to join Charlotte and Sharice!")
---
| Our two special guests guarantee a good time at this event, but there will be even more fun to be had! **We'll have musical performances, drag shows, mixologists, and so much more! **

We might not be able to get out and celebrate Pride in person this year, but we can still have a great time!

Wear your Pride best (or rainbow sweats, whatever, we’re not here to judge), pour yourself a cocktail - keep the bottle handy - and join us for the at-home celebration of the summer!

I hope to see you there!

Dani Brzozowski
Democratic Nominee for Congress, IL-16
---
| [Reserve your spot at Pride!](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=c7a59e8154&e=140a9b81e7 "Reserve your spot at Pride!")

](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xV2zDxiT3viZAfJvBDzD0HGVMC8Qcws9Ac1x5p9lNZ0vTbJk9wTb_9xhiy9V_ZpQeUhmm5-t2d_jmbwM4D-CLr4XlW80n_6ey0ksejkR_hx16K8IKjncysK1vKgnK-Os243lqL71O1KRzjbG6AIBjCXGO1mCf6EqONzadqWfZceYMU_lOXU9_Js0vrWyMXcmTxgwp3J74HQdBZr97JnXiG42maOtCSYdb3tksdEk3pICxKo_YZm_hiJWh1HJvzi35vRlRVH15ahWaaMZpe8cPsFpos5l8GCmAdoN74oU905w/32r/A24drxAzQ_mb9OBTxn2f3A/h0/Wx8KvlxcKKstjcnhchFewS3FjV8dqJNtZaPur33p6Xk) Friend, **** **** **** Today we are placing our final TV ad buy of the campaign.
Why are TV ad buys so important? Since we started spending heavily on TV ads, Mondaire has skyrocketed from a four-way tie for first to an 11-point lead. When voters get to know Mondaire, they choose him. With 24 percent of voters still undecided, we must keep growing our lead.
**_[We have to increase our ad buy this weekend to ensure we win on Election Day. disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greg Pence for Congress |
body:
The liberal playbook for 2020 is becoming clearer by the day – and it’s worse than we thought. Friend, Did you get Greg’s message the other day?
**The liberal playbook for 2020 is becoming clearer by the day – and it’s worse than we thought.**
Liberals won’t stop until our police departments are a thing of the past, late term abortions are legal in all 50 states, and lawful gun owners lose their 2nd Amendment rights.
**[Click here to join Greg in defense of our conservative American values against the radical liberal agenda we see unfolding today>>>](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=832a7c6315704f169db7dc8844c6983b&_e=jaPTcQlhqAmsTb0ObvU2F7FqM9ebFOa1nmD-JpZYe839nO_oXqNzRAPbDW6TQwYChaO5mCO1cFBHVeG5iclNSyv90YKIfQC1Jy723W02d3FhKJeaAjMBTSWlrJh0zE7gHSALbrr89HfJTGG1J0sNW-eBt_VtdM0q6CXM9sKVGcVn9XI5z0GuHD21_qqUhC0LroedZC89WICwu-P1AGF0eGKi9RJ1l036bsaXgW8_S3u3BTdbdWplmpvHidQ26Wrv0br9bLbGCvTcsPiGCgXsDYkLbSK_ka84qhTqStNkJS3z62yR42b6UkF3uoUNgxIr0ET0HQXmdVLB_1vgMsITC6dRWRY1sU8pcu51rVNReAVLiFqO81d4yx2zLU3BgrfZttYBi3YrUPKA-WSVlx8PMeNFZr0q9VgmqGbGNsRCKRo%3D)**
Greg asked us to find just 100 Hoosiers to take a stand, and we’re currently sitting just 34 short.
Will you help us bridge the gap? Can you chip in $10 to help us out? Our values are on the line right now, and they’ll certainly be on the line when we head to the polls on November 3rd. [**CHIP IN $10 TO DEFEND CONSERVATIVE VALUES**](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=832a7c6315704f169db7dc8844c6983b&_e=jaPTcQlhqAmsTb0ObvU2F7FqM9ebFOa1nmD-JpZYe839nO_oXqNzRAPbDW6TQwYChaO5mCO1cFBHVeG5iclNSyv90YKIfQC1Jy723W02d3FhKJeaAjMBTSWlrJh0zE7gHSALbrr89HfJTGG1J0sNW-eBt_VtdM0q6CXM9sKVGcVn9XI5z0GuHD21_qqUhC0LroedZC89WICwu-P1AGF0eEqbxR5q49kGRZ07t2prEKdthFUKd1qJrKVYE3Xq74PGtqln7w4dxxOni04354oHx5nvhrETOMWkqtW_aO3obM9L7k3F0__lX1d0zsKE_qff63C4rnuwdsddE4qzN4nRCcnsSIUVFMiVf2KCmz4wS-4YUoSS5VSxeZG00GTQqO3LKDihb-WV1tECnOC7RuWK_uZYUsJr2t5h1NdiTNHlh7Y%3D) Thank you for your support, Team Pence **\- - - Forwarded Message Below - - -** From: Greg Pence Subject: Everything’s on the line this year To: [dpwillis67@gmail.com](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=832a7c6315704f169db7dc8844c6983b&_e=jaPTcQlhqAmsTb0ObvU2F7FqM9ebFOa1nmD-JpZYe839nO_oXqNzRAPbDW6TQwYCrUZ5xWEdhM_tDFtxitJz4C6pZib9c6PJrMDWo97iGwsjBMZluqLuUh2ZY4U3xgrYvBa2xjn4J9ZEzdFWNZC36EzV-8CYse_MK3cQzxUy33XJ8Hh5cT6jPSlPCKGs0zYC6XeHtwHvtnCvtOxlhUFFT9yTV4Ac4SVgQwBg9AZLy_mdh62P8X5ic1j_CSn1-R9EtGAZnfe1cW5Z1PAO8maS79ijqyXELOBTg3j2OAHBSQvR9fI-lrz0vKtffWV6T2fpSkyMKMkvYDvOkEFQ48imuxjhgLZMcDE83QcxMRGQvDmNgkaJqOrg_Wj6Qty_V7zSJNkuoTAIhC3Te6uDNTIBQTkPxfTWyaWTDXMimCk3mXY%3D) [ ](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=832a7c6315704f169db7dc8844c6983b&_e=jaPTcQlhqAmsTb0ObvU2F7FqM9ebFOa1nmD-JpZYe839nO_oXqNzRAPbDW6TQwYCrUZ5xWEdhM_tDFtxitJz4C6pZib9c6PJrMDWo97iGwsjBMZluqLuUh2ZY4U3xgrYvBa2xjn4J9ZEzdFWNZC36EzV-8CYse_MK3cQzxUy33XJ8Hh5cT6jPSlPCKGs0zYCitCGGoDPtdgxSY0eUnTx6CvPL2w8YQnnlqJqwJz7rBE-mQQz2Giq0naVC-3mJxBENytg4j1dIIGsXc4ty381nobJ354XuZQNJhkv2BH-2AJtUyxeGA0opL256njxOf-qU5vlc28-Z_GR7duxA69gG610ieb7XCjBXLvV2cmul0gzJ7X-Ohxi_-446n8MJvG4LkzqNnZh8UvDhQPYG3IPHOKdHySsr0EIUU-MG9AnFxM%3D) Friend, I can’t overstate just how crucial the next few months will be for our nation. When we head to the polls in November, we won’t just be deciding the future of the Presidency and Congress.
**There is so much more than that.**
We’ll be deciding the future of law enforcement, abortion, and gun rights in America. These are issues that define who we are as a country, and we can’t afford to get this wrong.
**Our conservative Hoosier values are being threatened by anarchists and radicals on the Left right now, and I’m looking for just 100 supporters to stand with me and fight back.** [**CHIP IN $10 TO DEFEND CONSERVATIVE VALUES**](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=832a7c6315704f169db7dc8844c6983b&_e=jaPTcQlhqAmsTb0ObvU2F7FqM9ebFOa1nmD-JpZYe839nO_oXqNzRAPbDW6TQwYChaO5mCO1cFBHVeG5iclNSyv90YKIfQC1Jy723W02d3FhKJeaAjMBTSWlrJh0zE7gHSALbrr89HfJTGG1J0sNW-eBt_VtdM0q6CXM9sKVGcVn9XI5z0GuHD21_qqUhC0LroedZC89WICwu-P1AGF0eFvklk8bCxOjRyAuTtesMG6CCQdC0SHXni7_I3M0-_VmDz1-YEIdtiRUJasuUHKO_F9pCttod6Rplh87BmAKvwrDZCZgB4kshFd4_JrJstMqBTRlDDkhd4W_XnRVlCk0lCfdve6EuWeQnVOBE8F3_CPGbRs_tU6ehG6aR8SyblFnQqsAAo9XgUtmD91UIJbAAfLO4iP-OGq4Z5AiqfaXs8w%3D) Friend, the Democratic agenda for 2020 and beyond is becoming crystal-clear right now. They want to defund our police departments, dismantle our 2nd Amendment, and legalize late-term abortions in all 50 states.
This isn’t what the America I grew up in stands for, and I’m fighting every day in Washington to make sure that our conservative values aren’t forgotten.
**Will you fight alongside me today? I’m looking for just 100 Hoosiers to chip in $10 and come to the defense of our conservative values, which are under attack right now.** [**CHIP IN $10 TO DEFEND CONSERVATIVE VALUES**](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=832a7c6315704f169db7dc8844c6983b&_e=jaPTcQlhqAmsTb0ObvU2F7FqM9ebFOa1nmD-JpZYe839nO_oXqNzRAPbDW6TQwYChaO5mCO1cFBHVeG5iclNSyv90YKIfQC1Jy723W02d3FhKJeaAjMBTSWlrJh0zE7gHSALbrr89HfJTGG1J0sNW-eBt_VtdM0q6CXM9sKVGcVn9XI5z0GuHD21_qqUhC0LroedZC89WICwu-P1AGF0eBTarEmmyoU_s1PB-Ukk4xMQySo07pOh5i1cLaqr3fP9QDNykODYFBjSlp9xizBE9AWdKhXN9cS74k554KwHgeMo1CAYWFygeDFmg1c4bVaYIJbqreknBEvgiXCsWSp7cauV-47kTuEa0vJAGF7sGJKrHnPBFOp4fVvcgEjcbR0XmYFQCfoKe6pXHRuhzOPE8aNmDAg5OUVvl3vDz8xjhRM%3D) I’m proud to stand for what’s right in Congress - even if it’s not politically correct.
Thank you for your continued support, Greg Pence |

An individual may not contribute more than a total of $2,800 (maximum for Primary Election) per election to the committee. Federal law prohibits contributions to the committee from the general treasury funds of a corporation, labor organizations or national banks (including corporate or other business entity credit cards), from any person contributing another’s funds, from a Federal government contractor, or from a foreign national who lacks permanent resident status.

Thank you for your continued support, Greg Pence | Federal election law requires political committees to use best efforts to report the name, mailing address, occupation, and name of employer of individuals whose contributions exceed $200 during an election cycle. An individual may not contribute more than a total of $2,800 (maximum for Primary Election) per election to the committee. Federal law prohibits contributions to the committee from the general treasury funds of a corporation, labor organizations or national banks (including corporate or other business entity credit cards), from any person contributing another’s funds, from a Federal government contractor, or from a foreign national who lacks permanent resident status. | | | Paid for by Greg Pence for Congress | | PO Box 275 Taylorsville, IN 47280 | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com | | |

name: Team Warner
subject: changing our policing system
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
Systemic racism in our policing system has been left unaddressed for far too long.
|
---

| | Derek --

Systemic racism in our policing system has been left unaddressed for far too long.

Democrats, led by the Congressional Black Caucus, introduced a sweeping police reform bill called the _Justice in Policing Act_ , which would make common-sense changes to hold police officers who break the law accountable, address unchecked bias, change the culture of law enforcement, and lay the groundwork for trust between law enforcement and our communities.

Mark Warner is proud to be an original co-sponsor of the _Justice in Policing Act_ , but it's critical that we demonstrate strong public support for these reforms so we can pass this bill. **[Will you add your name as a supporter?](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d435/53d9897e/54b759c8/1c8841e4/2623157511/VEsH/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://sign.actiontaker.org/page/s/cbc-ja-justice-in-policing-act?source=Warner&redirect_url=https://sign.actiontaker.org/CBC-Warner-AB")**

**There 's no one policy that will fix our policing system, but the _Justice in Policing Act_ is a good first step toward concrete changes.**

This critical legislation will:
* Create a national standard for use of force;
* Ban neck holds, chokeholds, and similar excessive force tactics;
* Ban no-knock warrants;
* Requires the use of body cams for police;
* Require local police departments to send data on the use of force to the federal government;
* Reform racial profiling training for police officers;
* Create a grant program that would allow state attorneys general to create an independent process to investigate misconduct or excessive use of force; and
* Limit qualified immunity, making it easier to hold police officers who violate the law accountable for their actions.

Proposals put forth by Republicans aren't going far enough. We need to show huge support for the _Justice in Policing Act_ so we can take steps toward meaningful reform. **[Will you sign our petition and show your support for the _Justice in Policing Act_?](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d435/53d9897e/54b759c8/1c8841e4/2623157511/VEsE/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://sign.actiontaker.org/page/s/cbc-ja-justice-in-policing-act?source=Warner&redirect_url=https://sign.actiontaker.org/CBC-Warner-AB")**

Thank you for your support.

-- Team Warner

---
|
---

| [](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d435/53d9897e/54b759c8/1c8841e3/2623157511/VEsF/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-db?refcode=email-footer-img&refcode2=cont&amount=10") **[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d435/53d9897e/54b759c8/1c8841e2/2623157511/VEsC/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-db?refcode=email-footer-db&refcode2=cont&amount=10")**

name: Eugene DePasquale
subject: Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by DePasquale for PA 10
body:
Today commemorates a crucial milestone in our nation’s history. On June 19, 1865, news of the end of the Civil War and the abolishment of slavery reached Galveston, Texas, freeing the last group of enslaved people in the United States. Now that day is known as Juneteenth, and is the oldest known holiday commemorating the end of slavery in the United States.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17936775/211447065/1726898098?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OWU3Y2IwYy01YmIyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1LfwwkfmAYFpq7JS247kPUrlQUklJyb2STEghRtj2dE=&emci=3537fc41-34b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=79e7cb0c-5bb2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=10203249)
Friend,

Today commemorates a crucial milestone in our nation’s history. On June 19, 1865, news of the end of the Civil War and the abolishment of slavery reached Galveston, Texas, freeing the last group of enslaved people in the United States. Now that day is known as Juneteenth, and is the oldest known holiday commemorating the end of slavery in the United States.

While this was a significant turning point for our country, we cannot celebrate Juneteenth without acknowledging how much work we have left to do to ensure full equality for Black Americans. 155 years later, systemic racism remains ingrained in our society, and we have fallen short as a nation that guarantees liberty and justice for all.

I hope you will stand with me today in commemorating Juneteenth and the progress we’ve made since, as well as join me in committing to fighting for a better, more just future for all.

Thank you,

Eugene
| __Eugene DePasquale is running for Congress to represent Pennsylvania's 10th district. To learn more, please visit [EugeneForCongress.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17936776/211447066/1960319551?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OWU3Y2IwYy01YmIyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1LfwwkfmAYFpq7JS247kPUrlQUklJyb2STEghRtj2dE=&emci=3537fc41-34b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=79e7cb0c-5bb2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=10203249). name: True the Vote
subject: True the Vote Weekly Update June 13-19
body:
| | | | |
---
| |
---
| |
---
|
---
| | WHAT HAPPENED THIS WEEK

138 days. That is how long we have to prepare for the 2020 Elections. We are incredibly grateful for the thousands of volunteers we have all over the country who are dedicated to helping uphold free and fair elections for every American, no matter their race, background, or ideology. Your work is more important now than ever. There is a frightening movement afoot that grows by the day to strike down the rule of law in numerous states across the country and further dismantle the already-vulnerable security measures meant to protect every American’s vote.

The only way we will reverse this course is by growing our ranks and making our voices heard - to election officials, to local, state, and federal lawmakers. We need them to know that we will not stand silent while bad actors seek to tear down election integrity. We need them to know that their constituents expect them to uphold the law and defend the basic rights guaranteed by our U.S. Constitution. We need YOU to take a stand.

There are many ways you can make a difference. [Please, sign up to volunteer](https://truethevote.acemlnc.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=63A96A14A516). Help us with our Call Your County project, take our election worker training, connect with others in your state. Urge your friends to join you, too. We need poll workers, election observers, and citizen activists all engaging in this election. The future of our country and the security of our election depends upon it.

Thank you for your support, and see you at the polls!

Onward!
Catherine Engelbrecht
---
| |
---
|
---
| | CATCH OUR LATEST PODCAST EPISODES OF RED, WHITE, & TRUE
---
| | | | |
---

Episode 4 | Continue to Serve with Ed Hiner [LISTEN NOW >](https://truethevote.acemlnc.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=63A96A14A520)
Episode 3 | Cygnal Pollster Brent Buchanan [LISTEN NOW >](https://truethevote.acemlnc.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=63A96A14A521)
Episode 2 | Election Assistance Commissioners [LISTEN NOW >](https://truethevote.acemlnc.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=63A96A14A522)
Episode 1 | TTV Attorney Jim Bopp [LISTEN NOW >](https://truethevote.acemlnc.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=63A96A14A523)

| | |
---
| |
---
|
---
| | NEWS TO KNOW

1\. There's a New Way to Report Voting Rights Violations The Department of Justice has announced the launch of the [Civil Rights Reporting Portal](https://truethevote.acemlnc.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=63A96A14A524). This new online tool will make it easier for the public to report a civil rights violation - which includes voting irregularities! If you believe your voting rights are being violated, [tell your story](https://truethevote.acemlnc.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=63A96A14A524) \- do not be silent!

2\. The Life of Our Electoral College is Being Decided by SCOTUS This month SCOTUS will decide the future of the Electoral College. [This article](https://truethevote.acemlnc.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=63A96A14A525) walks through how we got here and what happens next. Definitely keep your eyes on this issue!

3\. Former Top Election Official Warns of Mass Mail-In Vote Fiasco. Michigan's former Secretary of State Ruth Johnson is joined by a chorus of MI election clerks sounding the alarm about [mail-in ballots and the risk to election integrity.](https://truethevote.acemlnc.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=63A96A14A526) These are the people in the trenches and they know of what they speak.

name: Nicole Malliotakis
subject: keep it up

| | **-June 19, 2020-** **-Jonathan Weinzapfel: the next Attorney General of Indiana-** The results are in! Jonathan Weinzapfel is our 2020 Democratic Attorney General candidate! Congratulations, Mayor Weinzapfel! This victory is well-deserved. You can follow Mayor Weinzapfel's campaign on social media: [**Twitter**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937926/211475733/-179666001?emci=e677cfd5-d4b0-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=%7B%7BContactsEmailID%7D%7D&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=), [**Facebook**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937927/211475735/1665464058?emci=e677cfd5-d4b0-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=%7B%7BContactsEmailID%7D%7D&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=), and [**Instagram**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937928/211475736/1572983100?hl=en&emci=e677cfd5-d4b0-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=%7B%7BContactsEmailID%7D%7D&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=)! Or check out his website [**here**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937929/211475737/2013185870?emci=e677cfd5-d4b0-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=%7B%7BContactsEmailID%7D%7D&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=). 2020 is the year we vote out Curtis Hill, turn back the attacks on Hoosiers' health care and restore integrity to the Attorney General's office. Jonathan Weinzapfel is the candidate to do it! Thank you to Senator Tallian for running an inspired campaign of ideas. Watch Mayor Weinzapfel's acceptance speech [**_here_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937930/211475738/-803653937?v=HFJ8iBeAQIs&feature=youtu.be&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=&emci=06af37d4-31b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=f2080877-67b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=4925535). **-The GOP's cringeworthy bid for AG-** [****](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937931/211475739/-1538199371?refcode=FFF6.19.20&amount=7&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=) Last night, the GOP held their 2020 Convention Preview. The broadcast, designed to showcase their four (yes, four) Attorney General candidates, turned into a bare-knuckled takedown of Curtis Hill and a clear indicator that they won't win the AG race in November. Hill's disgraceful [criminal battery charges](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937932/211475740/383756066?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=&emci=06af37d4-31b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=f2080877-67b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=4925535), his misuse of [taxpayer dollars](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937933/211475741/-2006356931?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=&emci=06af37d4-31b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=f2080877-67b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=4925535) for grandiose office renovations, and the [dwindling likelihood](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937934/211475742/-1004139039?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=&emci=06af37d4-31b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=f2080877-67b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=4925535) of him keeping the seat in November were all on the menu. Todd Rokita, the Party's other frontrunner, is the only candidate to [lose the race](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937935/211475743/1271561440?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=&emci=06af37d4-31b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=f2080877-67b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=4925535) to replace Mike Pence as governor AND lose the 2018 Indiana Senate primary AND be [drawn out of his legislative district](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937936/211475744/1331870872?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=&emci=06af37d4-31b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=f2080877-67b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=4925535) by his own Party. But each of their candidates had two crucial things in common: the unwavering desire to strip Hoosiers of their health care and rob us of our right to choose. The good news is the Cook Political Report moved the Indiana Attorney General's race from "leans Republican" to a toss-up. We created our [Fire Curtis Hill Fund](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937937/211475745/-1538199371?refcode=FFF6.19.20&amount=7&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=) to back the campaign to flip his seat. Every dollar goes toward getting Dems voting in November to protect our rights, save our health care and bring integrity back to the AG's office. **[Will you give $7 to help us hit the ground running?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937938/211475746/1782120907?refcode=FFF6.19.20&amount=7&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=)** **-This week's SCOTUS victories- ** **** The last four days have brought two major victories in the US Supreme Court. On Monday, the SCOTUS ruled that the 1964 Civil Rights Act [protects gay, lesbian, and transgender employees from discrimination](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937939/211475747/821099510?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=&emci=06af37d4-31b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=f2080877-67b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=4925535) based on sex. Yesterday, the Court [blocked Trump from ending DACA](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937940/211475748/-929158875?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=&emci=06af37d4-31b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=f2080877-67b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=4925535). The 5-4 ruling maintains the Obama-era program that protects immigrants brought to the US as children from deportation. For those affected, these rulings are affirmations that their jobs, their homes, and their humanity will live to see another day. _This_ is what we fight for. But the work isn't done yet. The DACA ruling was based on the [procedural requirements](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937941/211475749/-929158875?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=&emci=06af37d4-31b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=f2080877-67b2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=4925535) to end the program. This means Trump can still terminate it if he can take the necessary steps. Similarly, the basic rights of the LBGTQ+ community are at stake if Trump gets another SCOTUS pick. It's essential that we solidify these rulings at the ballot box in November. We can't let Trump take them back. **-Juneteenth-** **** One hundred fifty-five years ago, the last of the enslaved Black population in Galveston, Texas, received word that they were officially free. This year's Juneteenth comes just 25 days after the tragic murder of George Floyd. In these 25 days, Americans have fought hard against the racism pervading not only our criminal justice system but also our health care, schools, and businesses. The perennial fight of Black Americans shows us that the existence of freedom does not temper the absence of equality. If we are genuinely striving for a more perfect union, we must not let their fight for equality fall upon deaf ears. So, while today is a day to celebrate freedom, it's also vital that we don't lose sight of the future. -Resources- **Sign our petition** to stand up for Black Hoosiers **[here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937942/211475750/-1118395748?emci=297a1915-fbab-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=4925535&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=)**. **Read our blog post** on how to be an ally to Black and brown communities **[here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17937943/211475751/2016940711?emci=297a1915-fbab-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=4925535&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjA4MDg3Ny02N2IyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5H4aZopc7r5bwwAQYCNkdxsOjN2_oEKf1RJNtvnqEWc=)**. **Sign our petition** to call on the Indiana Election Division to make the 2020 general election a no-fault absentee election for all Hoosier voters clean_body: | | | | ---|--- | | -June 19, 2020- -Jonathan Weinzapfel: the next Attorney General of Indiana- The results are in! Jonathan Weinzapfel is our 2020 Democratic Attorney General candidate! Congratulations, Mayor Weinzapfel! This victory is well-deserved. You can follow Mayor Weinzapfel's campaign on social media: =, =, and =! Or check out his website =. 2020 is the year we vote out Curtis Hill, turn back the attacks on Hoosiers' health care and restore integrity to the Attorney General's office. Jonathan Weinzapfel is the candidate to do it! Thank you to Senator Tallian for running an inspired campaign of ideas. Watch Mayor Weinzapfel's acceptance speech . -The GOP's cringeworthy bid for AG- = Last night, the GOP held their 2020 Convention Preview. The broadcast, designed to showcase their four (yes, four) Attorney General candidates, turned into a bare-knuckled takedown of Curtis Hill and a clear indicator that they won't win the AG race in November. Hill's disgraceful , his misuse of for grandiose office renovations, and the of him keeping the seat in November were all on the menu. Todd Rokita, the Party's other frontrunner, is the only candidate to to replace Mike Pence as governor AND lose the 2018 Indiana Senate primary AND be by his own Party. But each of their candidates had two crucial things in common: the unwavering desire to strip Hoosiers of their health care and rob us of our right to choose. The good news is the Cook Political Report moved the Indiana Attorney General's race from "leans Republican" to a toss-up. We created our = to back the campaign to flip his seat. Every dollar goes toward getting Dems voting in November to protect our rights, save our health care and bring integrity back to the AG's office. = -This week's SCOTUS victories- ** The last four days have brought two major victories in the US Supreme Court. On Monday, the SCOTUS ruled that the 1964 Civil Rights Act based on sex. Yesterday, the Court . The 5-4 ruling maintains the Obama-era program that protects immigrants brought to the US as children from deportation. For those affected, these rulings are affirmations that their jobs, their homes, and their humanity will live to see another day. This is what we fight for. But the work isn't done yet. The DACA ruling was based on the to end the program. This means Trump can still terminate it if he can take the necessary steps. Similarly, the basic rights of the LBGTQ+ community are at stake if Trump gets another SCOTUS pick. It's essential that we solidify these rulings at the ballot box in November. We can't let Trump take them back. -Juneteenth- One hundred fifty-five years ago, the last of the enslaved Black population in Galveston, Texas, received word that they were officially free. This year's Juneteenth comes just 25 days after the tragic murder of George Floyd. In these 25 days, Americans have fought hard against the racism pervading not only our criminal justice system but also our health care, schools, and businesses. The perennial fight of Black Americans shows us that the existence of freedom does not temper the absence of equality. If we are genuinely striving for a more perfect union, we must not let their fight for equality fall upon deaf ears. So, while today is a day to celebrate freedom, it's also vital that we don't lose sight of the future. -Resources- Sign our petition to stand up for Black Hoosiers =. Read our blog post on how to be an ally to Black and brown communities =. Sign our petition to call on the Indiana Election Division to make the 2020 general election a no-fault absentee election for all Hoosier voters . -Save the date- Cass County Dems: \- Every Saturday, name: Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: Bad SCOTUS decision, Cops Calling In Sick, No Second Wave, and Trump Rallies Again
body:
**[Web version](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/m/1/49109489/02-b20171-3f34113c9174407a87e65e7e80b6e770/80/115/118cfe4f-7cbc-4e64-bc80-314e6d2b1243)** In a 5-4 decision this week, the Supreme Court ruled that the Trump Administration could not move forward in shutting down the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) program, as previously planned. Effectively asserting that while the Obama Administration started the program by executive action, the Trump Administration could not rescind it by executive action. Establishing a baffling and senseless legal double-standard. In his dissent, Justice Clarence Thomas accurately summed up what took place, “Today’s decision must be recognized for what it is: an effort to avoid a politically controversial but legally correct decision.” The facts surrounding the case were clear. The Trump Administration was in the right, but instead of making the legally correct decision, the highest court in the land chose to abandon the law for a baffling display of judicial activism. | |
---
On Friday, June 12, police in Atlanta, Georgia responded to a call of a man sleeping, in a parked vehicle, in a Wendy’s drive-thru lane.

After officers woke the man, later identified as Rayshard Brooks, they asked him to move his vehicle out of the drive-thru lane, before they had a conversation with him.

Following a roughly 41 minute conversation with Brooks, officers on scene moved to arrest him for DUI. It was at this point that Brooks became combative, resisting arrest, physically assaulting officers, and stealing one of their tasers.

As he attempted to flee the scene, he turned, aimed, and fired the taser at police, causing the pursuing officer to shoot and kill Rayshard Brooks.

After word of incident spread, protests erupted, which in some cases quickly devolved into riots. Coming on the heels of protests and riots across the nation following the death of George Floyd in police custody, many were calling for the officers involved to be charged.

Days after this, the Fulton County District Attorney, Paul Howard, announced 11 charges against the now former Atlanta Police Officer Garrett Rolfe, including felony murder.

His announcement was met with cheers by some and disbelief by others. It was nothing more than capitulation to the mob. It was reported that for the evening and morning shifts after the announcement, many Atlanta police officers called out sick in protest.

How could anyone be surprised by this? When an officer can be charged with felony murder for simply doing his job by the book, why would anyone want to be a police officer?
|
---
Earlier this week, we held a media call with doctors on the front lines of the fight against the coronavirus, who voiced support for Vice President Mike Pence's clear message:

_There will be no “second wave” of coronavirus._

Our Co-Founder Jenny Beth Martin was joined by Doctors Simone Gold, Mark McDonald, Lionel Lee, David Williams, and others. Together they conveyed the message that the reported rising infections in Texas, Arizona, Florida, Alabama and other states were not cause for alarm. Rather, that this rise in case numbers could be attributed to “increased testing and increased access to testing.”

**[See Politico coverage of the call here.](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/6/?T=NDkxMDk0ODk%3AMDItYjIwMTcxLTNmMzQxMTNjOTE3NDQwN2E4N2U2NWU3ZTgwYjZlNzcw%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR%3AZmFsc2U%3AMA%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cucG9saXRpY28uY29tL25ld3NsZXR0ZXJzL3BvbGl0aWNvLW5pZ2h0bHktY29yb25hdmlydXMtc3BlY2lhbC1lZGl0aW9uLzIwMjAvMDYvMTgvZG9udC1ibGFtZS10ZXN0aW5nLWZvci10aGUtc3VuLWJlbHQtc3Bpa2VzLTQ4OTU3NT9taWQ9NDIyMTgmaWQ9VDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR&K=4YAJP9iXT89u4RTRvGy_9w)**

[**POLITICO Nightly: Coronavirus Special Edition**](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/6/?T=NDkxMDk0ODk%3AMDItYjIwMTcxLTNmMzQxMTNjOTE3NDQwN2E4N2U2NWU3ZTgwYjZlNzcw%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR%3AZmFsc2U%3AMQ%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cucG9saXRpY28uY29tL25ld3NsZXR0ZXJzL3BvbGl0aWNvLW5pZ2h0bHktY29yb25hdmlydXMtc3BlY2lhbC1lZGl0aW9uLzIwMjAvMDYvMTgvZG9udC1ibGFtZS10ZXN0aW5nLWZvci10aGUtc3VuLWJlbHQtc3Bpa2VzLTQ4OTU3NT9taWQ9NDIyMTgmaWQ9VDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR&K=XjTmLFqC-SV3N3Zxp1TAkA)
Echoing arguments President Donald Trump has made in recent speeches and interviews, and advanced by Vice President Mike Pence in a Wall Street Journal op-ed this week, Tea Party-affiliated doctors held a call with reporters today... **[Read more...](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.org/c/6/?T=NDkxMDk0ODk%3AMDItYjIwMTcxLTNmMzQxMTNjOTE3NDQwN2E4N2U2NWU3ZTgwYjZlNzcw%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AVDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR%3AZmFsc2U%3AMg%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cucG9saXRpY28uY29tL25ld3NsZXR0ZXJzL3BvbGl0aWNvLW5pZ2h0bHktY29yb25hdmlydXMtc3BlY2lhbC1lZGl0aW9uLzIwMjAvMDYvMTgvZG9udC1ibGFtZS10ZXN0aW5nLWZvci10aGUtc3VuLWJlbHQtc3Bpa2VzLTQ4OTU3NT9taWQ9NDIyMTgmaWQ9VDE4bzlMbmV1akhoazkxUVFR&K=I7zaJ9LTiXnlvMvdZOsVUg)**
|
---
This weekend President Trump will officially re-start his campaign rallies in Tulsa, Oklahoma.

Not only is this great news for the President, it’s great news for the American people because it’s a sign we are finally returning to a sense of normalcy, following our months long battle with the coronavirus.

To no one's shock though, Democrats and the Mainstream Media are not happy about this return to campaigning. After weeks of cheering on protests, riots, and anarchists taking over city blocks, Democrats and the Mainstream Media are now attempting to say they are concerned with social distancing.

In their eyes it’s “social distancing for thee, but not for me,” apparently.

So, just to clarify their standard:

Thousands taking to the street to riot? No social distancing concerns.

Thousands wanting to peacefully assemble to support the President? The end of the world.

The fact is the Democrats and the Mainstream Media are beyond parody at this point. Americans are returning to a sense of normalcy, whether they like it or not. We wish they would drop their obvious double standard, but we know that will never happen.
|
---

|

name: Holcomb Crew
subject: Investing in our students
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
From the first day of his administration, Governor Holcomb has been committed to improving the quality of and access to education for all Hoosier... | | |
---|---|---
Friends,

This week, Governor Holcomb updated Hoosiers on the latest in Indiana’s response to COVID-19 and the continued impact the pandemic is having on our state.

As we safely and responsibly continue moving through the governor’s **[Back on Track plan](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=0aa903d4a78d47db82fbf94f052bfa7f&_e=7MaflwMUWHEeDkfZm76T1pkxCXJauB0FiHi_UaFBkQg5Y6KxtLZUkuIL6DmfBS50rgJxTJxA_UctyjqMzwcnSC5vkAMBag-7R2tdIPH3eRPuoLjESkIDqRRKhWuXY36uPsz3Ub0SD4Anvukvw0faDPYRXgPgTGAKhzYGXNes8yBmIqiBHSIvKAPecpqGrM5RF_UVjmCY8DOnfbvRzE7IbWarTDLJdmSTTeUs6c9wHq_IpSv8b8p4Xv0WYRGOIo_IKHQcY03PDR-ijOzOXdu_X9BX-bKpt2HsWm5EBADG8ONOEKTQPUIk9iT4z5w41K3nNZfFxsVbi27oNf1IyxPpuXmriaXnHkKMH9a7BVk7T1IygHNQ9XiZDx298JeDkth4)** , it can’t be forgotten that the pandemic disrupted the education of over 1 million Hoosiers. One of Governor Holcomb’s top priorities is strengthening education, and that’s why he is committed to maintaining the record level of K-12 investments for our students.

**So, while the pandemic will necessitate budget cuts in several state government agencies, Governor Holcomb will not cut K-12 funding to make up for revenue shortfalls due to the pandemic. In fact, a scheduled increase of $183 million will continue!**

Listen to what Governor Holcomb had to say about this great news:
[ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=0aa903d4a78d47db82fbf94f052bfa7f&_e=7MaflwMUWHEeDkfZm76T1gMAhpmAcOuIXVMJey4_XBdKz_0POl_8G0FoT_3OI0Y7YL0SCNLysL8qVf5hh8rWWkLW5aDgfsWb9HTpI1SXmTEHYtGy-CiZhw9qfY0Zm2P7BW8A2Pw4LxRuOFSwtP97mb5fh0BnRuUP1bV-kjGHPNFYmz3R31KIQMZKCfS8rWNFIgtRdHxkYjJ1GVXf2wgEmbvdZmSqN6tjHOuQR0_znX3T2wSlulh_VQCC2t_ZzPxM1BAd8GGTd1ZQEGDlAgAVeBXrzt_WaTtwXtUpJRhgjjcxRwy1nwyjvQtHBGFAUoSUwOMPqac-L3gkROjLUYeXo6XIoXKzKeq_IjnFH3ZraFnb7G0-l6VDn_-0ylH6dWcUUJt9Ef_B1O50HORxngrkMDu1IckSZ4uHBimNoovpUrY%3D)

Here are some additional updates regarding education funding:
* Schools are receiving an additional $192 million in federal funding from the CARES Act.
* Governor Holcomb’s education relief fund will provide $61 million for remote learning resources.
* Governor Holcomb will seek legislative approval to ensure schools that shifted to virtual instruction during the pandemic will still receive 100% of their tuition support.
* Additional resources and funding are available for schools through the Indiana Department of Homeland Security.

From the first day of his administration, Governor Holcomb has been committed to improving the quality of and access to education for all Hoosier students. While the pandemic has certainly caused some disruptions in our daily lives, it hasn’t changed his commitment to Hoosier teachers, parents and students.

name: Sri Preston Kulkarni
subject: Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sri for Congress
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17938465/211495432/-628849798?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjk1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYjM0YmJmMy02ZGIyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3OoD9B32ExqsSX4n7npPwlCJvIA_xYQF3Ram0mq_-W0=&emci=b3a1edc2-4db2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=fb34bbf3-6db2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=14145524)
---
| Hello all,

Today is Juneteenth, which celebrates the end of slavery in the United States. On this day, in 1865, federal troops arrived in Galveston, Texas to take control of the state and ensure that all enslaved people were freed.

Most of us grow up being taught that Abraham Lincoln’s Emancipation Proclamation freed all the slaves in America. In reality, the decree only applied to slaves in Confederate states, leaving slaves in border states or rebel areas in shackles. And even when emancipation was granted in these areas, slave owners often withheld news of freedom from slaves.

Union troops’ arrival on the 19th of June, 1865, and the fulfillment of the Emancipation Proclamation for slaves here in Texas came a full two and a half years after the document had been signed. Over a century and a half later, the gap between our laws and the reality of systemic racism in our society still cripples America.

Despite having a Declaration of Independence that declares all men are created equal, the 14th Amendment, and a Civil Rights Act, African Americans are incarcerated at five times the rate of their white counterparts, and Black women are three to four times more likely to die from childbirth than white women.

As a nation, we should be proud of the ideals we were founded on, but we should also recognize where we fall short of our ideals. Juneteenth is a day to remember the difference between actions and words, and for all allies to reflect on how to bridge that divide, in our criminal justice system, economy, and health care system.

Making Juneteenth a national holiday would both raise awareness of the day’s historical significance and give our society a moment to reflect on how we can all do more to help our America come closer to realizing its ideals.

If you’re curious where to get started, please visit our campaign anti-racism research page: [**https://sri2020.com/issues/antiracism/**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17938466/211495433/-628849798?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjk1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYjM0YmJmMy02ZGIyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3OoD9B32ExqsSX4n7npPwlCJvIA_xYQF3Ram0mq_-W0=&emci=b3a1edc2-4db2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=fb34bbf3-6db2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=14145524)

Sincerely,
Sri Preston Kulkarni

---
|

**Sri Preston Kulkarni** is a lifelong public servant and national security expert who has spent his career overseas working to protect Americans here at home. Growing up in Houston, Sri personally experienced the same challenges that our communities are facing today. In Congress, he’ll fight to lower the cost of prescription drugs, stand up to the gun lobby, address climate change, and bring common-sense solutions to Washington.
* * *
Bringing true representation to TX-22. Learn more at [sri2020.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17938467/211495434/307941387?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjk1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYjM0YmJmMy02ZGIyLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3OoD9B32ExqsSX4n7npPwlCJvIA_xYQF3Ram0mq_-W0=&emci=b3a1edc2-4db2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=fb34bbf3-6db2-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=14145524)

Over a century and a half later, the gap between our laws and the reality of systemic racism in our society still cripples America. Despite having a Declaration of Independence that declares all men are created equal, the 14th Amendment, and a Civil Rights Act, African Americans are incarcerated at five times the rate of their white counterparts, and Black women are three to four times more likely to die from childbirth than white women. As a nation, we should be proud of the ideals we were founded on, but we should also recognize where we fall short of our ideals. Juneteenth is a day to remember the difference between actions and words, and for all allies to reflect on how to bridge that divide, in our criminal justice system, economy, and health care system. Making Juneteenth a national holiday would both raise awareness of the day’s historical significance and give our society a moment to reflect on how we can all do more to help our America come closer to realizing its ideals. If you’re curious where to get started, please visit our campaign anti-racism research page: Sincerely, Sri Preston Kulkarni | Sri Preston Kulkarni is a lifelong public servant and national security expert who has spent his career overseas working to protect Americans here at home. Growing up in Houston, Sri personally experienced the same challenges that our communities are facing today. In Congress, he’ll fight to lower the cost of prescription drugs, stand up to the gun lobby, address climate change, and bring common-sense solutions to Washington. * Bringing true representation to TX-22. Learn more at | Paid for by Sri for Congress SRI FOR CONGRESS P.O. BOX 898 SUGAR LAND, TX 77487-0898 If you wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

name: Senator Patty Murray
subject: Mike Espy, Jaime Harrison, and Rev. Raphael Warnock
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY PEOPLE FOR PATTY MURRAY
body:
#

Team,
There have only been ten Black Senators in the entire history of the U.S. Senate.
Today, there are three -- Democrats Cory Booker and Kamala Harris, and Republican Tim Scott.
It is unacceptable that in a country as diverse as the U.S., our governing bodies simply don't reflect the lives and experiences of so many of our communities.
This November, we have the opportunity to take some steps toward correcting this representation problem by electing incredible Black leaders to the Senate.
**[I'm supporting Democrats Mike Espy in Mississippi, Jaime Harrison in South Carolina, and Rev. Raphael Warnock in Georgia. Join me and contribute $5 to their campaigns.](https://act.pattymurray.?g=KnQfVcQot1BdNp6fCr3xM1A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pm-200618-blackleaders-slate?refcode=em200618i&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=98208&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Everett&state=WA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**
Mike Espy made history as Mississippi's first Black congressman since Reconstruction, and was the first Black U.S. Secretary of Agriculture. Now Mike is building a grassroots campaign to take Mississippi forward, and he needs our support.
Jaime Harrison is a former chair and first Black person to lead the South Carolina Democratic Party -- and now he's building a competitive campaign around his vision of putting the American Dream within reach for more people in his state and across the country. With our help, he can win this race.
Rev. Raphael Warnock is the senior pastor at Ebenezer Baptist Church in Atlanta, the same pulpit from which Martin Luther King, Jr. once preached. Now, he's running for Senate because the people of Georgia deserve a real voice and a Senator who understands their struggles.
**If we win these races in November, we can not only take control of the Senate from the Republicans, but also bring needed Black leaders and voices to the Senate.**
**[Pitch in $5 now to help elect these Black Democratic leaders to the U.S. Senate.](https://act.pattymurray.?g=KnQfVcQot1BdNp6fCr3xM1A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pm-200618-blackleaders-slate?refcode=em200618i&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=98208&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Everett&state=WA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**
Thank you,
Patty

**[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.pattymurray.?g=KnQfVcQot1BdNp6fCr3xM1A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pm-200618-blackleaders-slate?refcode=em200618i&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=98208&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Everett&state=WA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**

_Title and affiliation are provided for identification purposes only._

**_As Washington's senior U.S. Senator, Patty Murray is working to help families, solve problems, and defend our progressive values._**
* * *
PAID FOR BY PEOPLE FOR PATTY MURRAY
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most critical tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can click here to let us know if you would like to [unsubscribe](https://act.pattymurray.com/page/m/a693216/22080b68/7a9c7323/46020b41/3467189337/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/?g=KnQfVcQot1BdNp6fCr3xM1A "http://act.pattymurray.com/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren Democrats
body:
On June 19th 1865, Galveston, Texas erupted in joy when news of emancipation finally arrived more than two years after Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation. That day is known as Juneteenth... | | | [](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852577c/45c30650/1c97fe166/66e9ef19/1101350620/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
On June 19th, 1865, Galveston, Texas erupted in joy when news of emancipation finally arrived more than two years after Abraham Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation. That day is known as Juneteenth: the annual and oldest known celebration commemorating the end of slavery in the United States.

Juneteenth is a chance to tell the resilient, persistent, bold and beautiful story of African Americans. How even in the face of oppression, Black Americans throughout our history have never stopped imagining and fighting for a better tomorrow. Not just for themselves, but for our entire nation.

But Juneteenth isn't just about celebration. It's a necessary reminder that 155 years later, Black Americans still feel the weight of government-sponsored racism and discrimination devaluing their lives and livelihoods.

Over the last few weeks, people have poured into the streets here in America and around the world to protest police violence against Black lives.

People are pouring into the streets because racist violence and white supremacy are still being used as tools of oppression to devalue and destroy Black lives. The tools of oppression come in all shapes and sizes, but today, on Juneteenth, I want to name three we must destroy.

**The first tool** is violence against Black lives.

We need real accountability for anyone -- including law enforcement officers -- responsible for the unjustified killings of countless Black Americans, and for any excessive use of force during peaceful protests.

We need to rethink our approach to public safety to focus less on locking people up and more on community investments to lift people up.

And we need to vote out a president who glorifies violence. He told us who he was, and now he's showing us who he is.

**The next tool of oppression** is voter suppression.

Racist politicians have used racist voter suppression laws to steal votes from people of color for generations. Partisan gerrymandering allows politicians to pick their voters instead of the other way around. Enough.

We need a constitutional amendment guaranteeing every United States citizen the right to vote and have that vote counted. We need to restore the Voting Rights Act, overturn every racist voter suppression law, and end partisan gerrymandering.

**Another tool of oppression** is economic inequality rooted in generations of deliberate discrimination and racism.

To ensure Black Americans have the opportunity to build wealth and build a future, we need to confront the racial wealth gap head on. And yes, that means providing assistance to first-time homebuyers who live in formerly redlined neighborhoods. But it also means cancelling student loan debt, funding Historically Black Colleges and Universities, investing in small businesses and giving Black entrepreneurs the resources they need.

And there is so much more we need to do to root out racial injustice in our health care system, our educational system, our housing policies, in the workplace, and in every part of our society.

I have not personally experienced and can never truly understand the fear, the oppression, and the pain that confronts Black Americans. But none of us can ignore what is happening in this country.

I understand the responsibility that I and others have as white Americans to come together with all justice-loving people and ask what we are doing to dismantle a system that has too often undervalued Black life. We cannot just be allies. We must be anti-racists.

So today on Juneteenth, and every day, let us recommit ourselves to defending the unshakeable truth that Black people matter, Black families matter, Black lives matter.

-- Elizabeth
