clean_body: On June 19th 1865, Galveston, Texas erupted in joy when news of emancipation finally arrived more than two years after Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation. That day is known as Juneteenth... | | | "" On June 19th, 1865, Galveston, Texas erupted in joy when news of emancipation finally arrived more than two years after Abraham Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation. That day is known as Juneteenth: the annual and oldest known celebration commemorating the end of slavery in the United States. Juneteenth is a chance to tell the resilient, persistent, bold and beautiful story of African Americans. How even in the face of oppression, Black Americans throughout our history have never stopped imagining and fighting for a better tomorrow. Not just for themselves, but for our entire nation. But Juneteenth isn't just about celebration. It's a necessary reminder that 155 years later, Black Americans still feel the weight of government-sponsored racism and discrimination devaluing their lives and livelihoods. Over the last few weeks, people have poured into the streets here in America and around the world to protest police violence against Black lives. People are pouring into the streets because racist violence and white supremacy are still being used as tools of oppression to devalue and destroy Black lives. The tools of oppression come in all shapes and sizes, but today, on Juneteenth, I want to name three we must destroy. The first tool is violence against Black lives. We need real accountability for anyone -- including law enforcement officers -- responsible for the unjustified killings of countless Black Americans, and for any excessive use of force during peaceful protests. We need to rethink our approach to public safety to focus less on locking people up and more on community investments to lift people up. And we need to vote out a president who glorifies violence. He told us who he was, and now he's showing us who he is. The next tool of oppression is voter suppression. Racist politicians have used racist voter suppression laws to steal votes from people of color for generations. Partisan gerrymandering allows politicians to pick their voters instead of the other way around. Enough. We need a constitutional amendment guaranteeing every United States citizen the right to vote and have that vote counted. We need to restore the Voting Rights Act, overturn every racist voter suppression law, and end partisan gerrymandering. Another tool of oppression is economic inequality rooted in generations of deliberate discrimination and racism. To ensure Black Americans have the opportunity to build wealth and build a future, we need to confront the racial wealth gap head on. And yes, that means providing assistance to first-time homebuyers who live in formerly redlined neighborhoods. But it also means cancelling student loan debt, funding Historically Black Colleges and Universities, investing in small businesses and giving Black entrepreneurs the resources they need. And there is so much more we need to do to root out racial injustice in our health care system, our educational system, our housing policies, in the workplace, and in every part of our society. I have not personally experienced and can never truly understand the fear, the oppression, and the pain that confronts Black Americans. But none of us can ignore what is happening in this country. I understand the responsibility that I and others have as white Americans to come together with all justice-loving people and ask what we are doing to dismantle a system that has too often undervalued Black life. We cannot just be allies. We must be anti-racists. So today on Juneteenth, and every day, let us recommit ourselves to defending the unshakeable truth that Black people matter, Black families matter, Black lives matter. -- Elizabeth | | | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" ---|---|---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren Democrats All content (C) 2020 Warren Democrats, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02117

name: Team Messner
subject: This Week: our new TV ad, Veterans Coalition Update, and more
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MESSNER FOR SENATE
body:
Tap or click to read more >>
Entrepreneur. Veteran. Conservative. [Contact Us](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/7cb5eaeabf107a2b/3781736cfe46c268c3d6b838967d376c/33153) | |
---
**Campaign Debuts New TV Ad Spotlighting Support for Small Business**
A television ad debuted this week spotlighting Corky Messner’s support of small business.

The **[second ad](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/7cb5eaeabf107a2b/466670ec8ca9c412c3d6b838967d376c/33153)** of the campaign focuses on restoring the economy following the COVID-19 pandemic, and his continued support of Granite State businesses.

[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/7cb5eaeabf107a2b/5a96e4eedb34a7b5c3d6b838967d376c/33153)

Corky’s campaign to unseat incumbent Democrat Jeanne Shaheen recently was endorsed by President Donald Trump, and he cites his support of the President in the new 30-second spot, as well as New Hampshire Governor Chris Sununu.

“Small businesses are the backbone of New Hampshire’s economy and they are getting back to work safely, thanks to President Trump and Governor Sununu,” he says.

Corky also points to his own business success. “I built a business from scratch. I know the challenges you’re facing. We need to bring back the economy so our families can preserve the American dream...D.C. politicians don’t believe in freedom and liberty. We do,” Messner says, promising to “fight for the Granite State in the U.S. Senate.”

The ad debuted Thursday on WMUR-TV Channel 9 and cable television and has a digital component.
|
---
**Veterans Coalition will be Advised by Retired 4-Star General Campbell and Lt. General Halverson and Led by State Rep. and Veterans Advocate Jess Edwards**
The campaign this week announced the formation of a coalition to connect with veterans, a group advised by a retired four-star general and a lieutenant general, and led by well-known New Hampshire veterans advocate and State Representative Jess Edwards.
Serving as national advisors will be retired four-star Army General John Campbell, former Vice Chief of Staff of the United States Army, and Lieutenant General David Halverson, who retired in 2016 from the Army after over 37 years of service. Both have endorsed Corky’s run to unseat Democrat incumbent Jeanne Shaheen.

Leading the veterans coalition will be Rep. Jess Edwards of Auburn, Rockingham-District 4, who is also announcing his endorsement. Edwards served 33 years in the U.S Army medical service corps, retiring as a Lieutenant Colonel in 2009. He is currently chairman of the General Court Veterans Interest Caucus, and has been active in veterans affairs across the state.

On the coalition steering committee with Edwards is retired Marine Corps Major Carol Moore of Hampton Falls, who served from the end of the Vietnam War through the end of the Cold War. She worked in Intelligence and Program Development in Washington, D.C. during the Reagan administration.

“I am privileged to have the support of these veterans as we continue to address the challenges facing the men and women who have proudly served their country,” says Corky. “I look forward to continuing to work on behalf of New Hampshire veterans and address the issues they face and the services they need in civilian life.”

More members will be joining the coalition in the coming weeks, with veterans-specific events and other initiatives being announced.
|
---
**Corky on the Road**
Keeping social distancing guidelines in mind, Corky attended the outdoor Merrimack County GOP meeting earlier in the week, and then joined a GOP meeting in Manchester. Campaigning is in full swing!
|
---
**Tune In Live or Catch Up!**
[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/7cb5eaeabf107a2b/921e273fbcd17593c3d6b838967d376c/33153)

Corky appears on Facebook via live feeds on a regular basis, but if you can’t join live, you can catch up on the latest from our candidate via **[our Facebook page](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/7cb5eaeabf107a2b/9a48a4d58786e211c3d6b838967d376c/33153)**. One **[recent video](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/7cb5eaeabf107a2b/09968feb76102b49c3d6b838967d376c/33153)** includes Corky talking about the President’s endorsement and its impact on his efforts to unseat Jeanne Shaheen.
(And while you’re on our FB page, check out Corky’s **[proud moment](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/7cb5eaeabf107a2b/83f80f845e056115c3d6b838967d376c/33153) **last weekend, when his son, Max, graduated from the U.S. Military Academy at West Point, with President Trump in attendance.)
|
---

name: =?utf-8?B?TGluZGEgU8OhbmNoZXo=?=
subject: The Justice in Policing Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stand with Sánchez |
body:
Given the nationwide protests against police brutality, now is the time for Congress to take concrete steps to ensure no more Black lives are lost due to unnecessary and unlawful to police violence. That's why I'm supporting the Justice in Policing Act, which proposes actionable reforms to change law enforcement for the better.

Will you join me by signing your name as a citizen co-sponsor of the Justice in Policing Act?

Like so many of you, I want to see a change in this country's law enforcement, especially when it comes to the way Black and Brown people are policed. George Floyd, Breonna Taylor, and Rayshard Brooks are just a few of the Black people who have been killed at the hands of police, but there are countless other names we'll never know.

We cannot go on like this. These lives mattered, and we must take action to make sure their deaths do not go unanswered.

Call for Police Reform | Demand Congress pass the Justice in Policing Act >>

Thank you,

-- Linda Sánchez

----------------------------------------
| Paid for by Stand with Sánchez |
----------------------------------------

Stand with Sánchez
415 New Jersey Avenue, SE - Unit 1
Washington DC 20003 United States

If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please https://act.myngp.com/el/V3FQsTkgBugNjkJU2Mk_uLiCEhHeV4aX7nvDGaMesG4=/QP2Arz5oclQOBM7sRXIaa3P-xqAOmvoUtrp74DvemRw=.

name: Support Vote By Mail (via VPP)
subject: Amy Klobuchar & Stacey Abrams
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Voter Protection Project and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee
body:
---
| | We are JUMPING FOR JOY!! [](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d041a/72412298/4d6a0ce3/61053685/1767099349/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/vpp_em_en_200530_vbmak_u2_x2?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200619_VBMAK_U2_X2&source=VPP_EM_EN_200619_VBMAK_U2_X2") [**Add your name to stand with Amy Klobuchar and Stacey Abrams to SUPPORT vote-by-mail! ->**](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d041a/72412298/4d6a0ce3/61053684/1767099349/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/vpp_em_en_200530_vbmak_u2_x2?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+to+stand+with+Amy+Klobucha&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200619_VBMAK_U2_X2&source=VPP_EM_EN_200619_VBMAK_U2_X2")
* * *
We are JUMPING FOR JOY!! **Voting Rights HERO Stacey Abrams is partnering with Senator Amy Klobuchar to EXPAND vote-by-mail ahead of the general election!!** This is FANTASTIC news for voters across the country. Amy Klobuchar is the lead sponsor of a new bill in the Senate that would protect our fundamental right to vote -- especially during the coronavirus pandemic. **[But we need to build more support for national vote-by-mail in order to make it a reality! Add your name to be one of the 50,000 signatures we need in the next 24 hours->](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d041a/72412298/4d6a0ce3/61053687/1767099349/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/vpp_em_en_200530_vbmak_u2_x2?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=3+-+But+we+need+to+build+more+support+for+na&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200619_VBMAK_U2_X2&source=VPP_EM_EN_200619_VBMAK_U2_X2")** | | | [Add Your Name](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d041a/72412298/4d6a0ce3/61053686/1767099349/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/vpp_em_en_200530_vbmak_u2_x2?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=4+-+Add+Your+Name&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200619_VBMAK_U2_X2&source=VPP_EM_EN_200619_VBMAK_U2_X2")
---
While Donald Trump and the GOP are spreading LIES about voting by mail, Amy Klobuchar is being a TOUGH critic of the dangerous GOP:
[](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d041a/72412298/4d6a0ce3/61053689/1767099349/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/vpp_em_en_200530_vbmak_u2_x2?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=5&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200619_VBMAK_U2_X2&source=VPP_EM_EN_200619_VBMAK_U2_X2")
YES! YES! YES!
**Amy Klobuchar and Stacey Abrams are the voting rights DREAM TEAM!!**
They're calling all Good Democrats to stand with them in support of vote-by-mail -- so we are BEGGING that you answer their call:
| | [Support vote by mail ->](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d041a/72412298/4d6a0ce3/61053688/1767099349/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/vpp_em_en_200530_vbmak_u2_x2?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=6+-+Support+vote+by+mail+rarr&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200619_VBMAK_U2_X2&source=VPP_EM_EN_200619_VBMAK_U2_X2")
---

(50,000 signatures needed in 24 hrs)
Thank you,
\-- the Voter Protection Project

| [Voter Protection Project](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d041a/72412298/4d6a0ce3/6105368b/1767099349/VEsB/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protectvotingorg?refcode=protectvotingorg_headerlogo&amount=25.00&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=7+-+Voter+Protection+Project&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200619_VBMAK_U2_X2&source=VPP_EM_EN_200619_VBMAK_U2_X2")

---

Friend,

I just reviewed an updated list of Official 2020 Trump Presidential Founders whose names will go down in history with us, and I noticed your name is STILL MISSING.

name: Nina Ahmad
subject: The votes are in!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nina for PA
body:
| | I am humbled to announce that I won the Democratic Nomination for Auditor General of Pennsylvania, having receiving 540,793 votes! I want to thank everyone who help [support](https://act.myngp.com/el/ladufP-ZWLAXFEWuzZJ442qVwhOvcEXcclOHSuoxuts=/VVLRszzAVvH975g8oo2sBBanP36UOHI-N2YMBbzPRGU=) me on my journey to this point as the Democratic Nominee. I truly could not have done this without your help! Today, I stand on the shoulders of giants as the first person of color, the first Bangladeshi- American woman to be nominated by Democrats for statewide executive office in Pennsylvania! I join all of you in celebrating Juneteenth and the resilience of those who broke the bonds that enslaved them. The road carved by them has allowed me to come this far as an immigrant and person of color. Although we can take a moment and celebrate this victory, I know that there is more work to do on the road to the November 3rd, 2020 General Election. I hope you will continue to engage with my campaign and support our journey to the General! **_Watch my Friday 's with Nina video series!_** [](https://act.myngp.com/el/ladufP-ZWLAXFEWuzZJ442qVwhOvcEXcclOHSuoxuts=/32LoYwxflTiDEdtWbaiW4IbZt60wH7mB5lJ-Ro-PYzg=?v=pYlv9Ca6wFk) Learn more by visiting our website: [**_www.NinaForPA.com_**](https://act.myngp.com/el/ladufP-ZWLAXFEWuzZJ442qVwhOvcEXcclOHSuoxuts=/cjaMCV3XFv9_n5eMHgiiNkWuH8YSJ0rGui8Uid2TyDw=) Follow us on Social Media: [](https://act.myngp.com/el/ladufP-ZWLAXFEWuzZJ442qVwhOvcEXcclOHSuoxuts=/jITX-OyjKK0OBD3D6X8DMwB5QLbHJOFHFa3EDjrQonk=) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/ladufP-ZWLAXFEWuzZJ442qVwhOvcEXcclOHSuoxuts=/N6wFJ9h0y2kzkGztwxxk-IE_eHQ-zCu3NG2vlPTLnnU=) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/ladufP-ZWLAXFEWuzZJ442qVwhOvcEXcclOHSuoxuts=/2niOupMbXP5ffti7CdUeXOlIP2sVdeof7R9GIE6nehY=?hl=en)
---

name: =?utf-8?Q?Outvote?=
subject: This Juneteenth, learn how your vote can promote racial justice
body:
Use your opportunity at the ballot box to continue to fight for progressive candidates and policies. | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/outvote.io/june-19-2020?e=0aad15100c)
---
| _Organizing for everyone._
---
| | | |
---
|
---
|
**Black Lives Matter**

---
| We join America in honoring Juneteenth today.
Juneteenth commemorates the end of slavery in the United States.
To learn more about the history of Juneteenth, click [here](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=a2ffc7a670&e=0aad15100c).

---
| [ ](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=5cd39e189d&e=0aad15100c)
---

We've spent all of 2020 getting ready for the biggest election year in our lifetimes, and now the fight is more urgent than ever. [In ](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=0f310dbb58&e=0aad15100c)[the latest from the Outvote blog](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=bac7614632&e=0aad15100c), learn how your vote can promote racial justice from your county all the way to the White House.

| [Learn More](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=2a15858f7d&e=0aad15100c "Learn More")
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| | | | **What We're Reading:**

---
| [ ](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=1f6041c30d&e=0aad15100c)
---
[**Dear White People: Now You Know and You Can’t Pretend You Don’t**](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=64a9f5c833&e=0aad15100c)

_6.19.20 | Medium_ Outvote's Mungi Ngomane on racism in America, Juneteenth, and nine ways for white people to confront injustice.
[ ](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=feb899ceb1&e=0aad15100c)
---
[**How to Get Involved, Politically**](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=c3183bcdaf&e=0aad15100c)

_6.9.20 | The New York Times_ Use Outvote to share voting and political information with your social network.

|
---
|
---
|
**What You Can Do: **

---
| | [ ](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=04ef31d25a&e=0aad15100c)
---
**#BlackTransLivesMatter**

Click [here](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=815c7a7f85&e=0aad15100c) for a document full of Actions and Resources for Solidarity, and click [here](https://outvote.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=dc952a46675058f5f7e2d3a0a&id=3576e9bef1&e=0aad15100c) to split a donation between a number of Black trans-led organizations.
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
|
### "Let today be the last day that you
ever doubt black trans power."
******\- Raquel Willis**
6.14.20 | Brooklyn Liberation March

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Five Stories President Trump Doesn’t Want You to Miss
body:
Assault on Open Speech is an Attack on America | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bbdfae7275&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **_FIVE STORIES PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | [**Assault on Open Speech is an Attack on America**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=431bded20e&e=3ee4985eea)
-_Washington Examiner_
“There are two sides in the defining fight ripping America apart, and they aren't Republicans and Democrats. The fight is between those who believe that open speech is essential to preserving the nation and those who believe in narrowing the debate and favor cultural erasure,” the _Washington Examiner_ editorial board writes.
---
|
---
| [**On Daca, Obama Can but Trump Can’t**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=30362a33bf&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Wall Street Journal_
“The practical consequence of the [Supreme Court’s DACA] ruling is that a President can create an unlawful policy without legislation from Congress, but a future President cannot lawfully undo it without first jumping through regulatory hoops that can take years,” _The Wall Street Journal_ editorial board writes.
---
|
---
| [**Trump Signs Police Reform Executive Order in Rose Garden Ceremony**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6263eb8919&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox News
“The National Fraternal Order of Police (FOP), the largest law enforcement union in the U.S., praised Trump's [executive order on policing],” Tyler Olsen reports. “‘It strikes a great balance between the vital need for public and officer safety, and the equally vital need for lasting, meaningful, and enforceable police reform,’ the organization said.”

**MORE:** [SAFE policing for SAFE communities!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=77153a94e1&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [**Trump Initiative Aims to Reduce High Suicide Rate Among Veterans**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1472d85158&e=3ee4985eea)
_-The Daily Signal_
This week, President Trump unveiled a new roadmap to address the issue of veteran suicide, Fred Lucas reports. “The loss of our heroes breaks our hearts and pains our souls . . . They fought our battle overseas, and now we must join them in winning this new battle at home,” the President said at a White House ceremony.

🎬 **WATCH** : [Second Lady Karen Pence on ending “stigma of mental health”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bb52f084cf&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [**Lockdowns Hit Minority Businesses**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=794e66223c&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The_ _Wall Street Journal_
Endless lockdowns hurt small businesses—and they may hit minority-owned businesses the hardest. “From February to April, the number of active black business owners fell 41%,” _The Wall Street Journal_ editorial board writes. Local leaders “should bear in mind who’d be harmed most by the ensuing economic destruction” of more lockdowns.
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a5d92cf592&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d1a98e999a&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=c660806737)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---
clean_body: Assault on Open Speech is an Attack on America | | | | | FIVE STORIES PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS | | | -Washington Examiner “There are two sides in the defining fight ripping America apart, and they aren't Republicans and Democrats. The fight is between those who believe that open speech is essential to preserving the nation and those who believe in narrowing the debate and favor cultural erasure,” the Washington Examiner editorial board writes. | | -The Wall Street Journal “The practical consequence of the [Supreme Court’s DACA] ruling is that a President can create an unlawful policy without legislation from Congress, but a future President cannot lawfully undo it without first jumping through regulatory hoops that can take years,” The Wall Street Journal editorial board writes. | | -Fox News “The National Fraternal Order of Police (FOP), the largest law enforcement union in the U.S., praised Trump's [executive order on policing],” Tyler Olsen reports. “‘It strikes a great balance between the vital need for public and officer safety, and the equally vital need for lasting, meaningful, and enforceable police reform,’ the organization said.” MORE: | | -The Daily Signal This week, President Trump unveiled a new roadmap to address the issue of veteran suicide, Fred Lucas reports. “The loss of our heroes breaks our hearts and pains our souls . . . They fought our battle overseas, and now we must join them in winning this new battle at home,” the President said at a White House ceremony. 🎬 WATCH : | | -The Wall Street Journal Endless lockdowns hurt small businesses—and they may hit minority-owned businesses the hardest. “From February to April, the number of active black business owners fell 41%,” The Wall Street Journal editorial board writes. Local leaders “should bear in mind who’d be harmed most by the ensuing economic destruction” of more lockdowns. | | | | | | The White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111

body:
Last night, we made history, with thousands of Hoosiers tuning in for our 2020 Indiana Republican State Convention. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/welcome-to-the-general-election-469385?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=aa4cd3e74a&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| [Full Video: Watch the 2020 Indiana Republican State Convention](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a98991a9a5&e=1acd9bb3d6 "Full Video: Watch the 2020 Indiana Republican State Convention")
---
|
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=35f63ca710&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_Making History_** Last night, we made history, with thousands of Hoosiers tuning in for our 2020 Indiana Republican State Convention.

While we all would have preferred to be together in person, I’m so excited that we had this opportunity to share our Republican message on this wide stage to so many of our fellow Hoosiers.

When I took the stage at our 2018 state convention, I made a commitment to grow this party and ensure that we reflect the tremendous diversity found in Indiana. I issued a call to action for everyone in our grassroots team to get involved in this mission. And last night, I gave an update on our progress.

I’m excited to share that we’re developing a statewide diversity series through the Indiana Republican Party to help identify, educate, train and invest in diverse Hoosiers who believe in the principles of the Republican Party. And we've got the perfect person on our team leading the charge. **Whitley Yates** , who joined our Indiana GOP team last December, is absolutely on fire, as you probably saw last night.

With her help, Indiana Republicans are currently building relationships in places our party has not been before. We’ll be announcing more details soon about our diversity series, so stay tuned.

Together, as Republicans we made history last night, we’re making history daily with our actions, and I know we’re ready to make more history together this November. \- Chairman Kyle Hupfer

**PHOTO** : **_Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer_** _ served as the emcee at last night's 2020 Indiana Republican State Convention. If you missed it, don't worry. You can [watch the full video here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=04890aa888&e=1acd9bb3d6). _
---
|
---
| | **Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5f1027c88a&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_One Indiana For All_** Last night, **Governor Holcomb** delivered the keynote address at our 2020 Indiana State Convention. Thanks to the hard work and determination of Hoosiers, we have Indiana in a position to come out of these difficult times faster and stronger than many of our neighbors.

_**"For us to be firing on all cylinders...we must cultivate this new economic and societal terrain to become One Indiana that provides opportunities for all." - Gov. Holcomb**_

Building One Indiana For All means:
* Getting Hoosiers back to work and connecting more Hoosiers with higher-wage jobs.
* Attracting more business leaders, job creators and entrepreneurs to Indiana.
* Working together to overcome racial inequality.
* Taking a long look at some of the restrictions and regulations we set aside during the pandemic to see which ones can go away permanently.
* Continuing to modernize your state government, making sure it is running efficiently for the taxpayers.
* Finishing I-69.
* Taking on long-lingering health issues.
* Hitting our goal of being the best state in the Midwest for infant mortality by 2024.
* Focusing on mental, physical and fiscal health.

_**"One Indiana For All... strengthened by our regions, our diversity, our communities, our history and most importantly, our people." - Gov. Holcomb**_ Through all the challenges this year have brought us, one thing has remained true. The Hoosier spirit has remained strong and resilient. It's going to take all of us working together to truly create One Indiana that provides opportunities for all!

To watch **Governor Holcomb's** full speech, click [here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f94a54a691&e=1acd9bb3d6).
---
|
---
| | [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=829ef10c6c&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_Celebrating Juneteenth_** Today we celebrate Juneteenth – the day when slavery finally came to an end in America, following President Lincoln’s Emancipation Proclamation almost two and a half years earlier.

Join us as we celebrate freedom and the fall of slavery in the United States, and we reflect on the importance of this day. As**Governor Holcomb** has said, “We don't have more time or lives to lose.”
---
|
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=600fd8696d&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_Delegates: Now It’s Time to Vote_** Last night, we hosted our history 2020 Indiana Republican State Convention. And now it’s time for our delegates to vote!

Delegates will begin receiving their ballots around June 22 -- **your ballot must arrive at the PO Box no later than July 9 at 5 p.m. ET.** If your ballot is received after that time it will not be counted. For more information about voting, [watch this video](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=baeda9f7dd&e=1acd9bb3d6) and [visit our convention website](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9b07f217aa&e=1acd9bb3d6).
---
|
---
| **_News You Can Use_** **Governor Eric Holcomb**[announces K-12 funding increase](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=83ec7ec166&e=1acd9bb3d6)[ of $183 million](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7f08ad1dc9&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch**[talks about the ongoing work to fight COVID-19](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=8ce93a7e09&e=1acd9bb3d6)[ in Indiana](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=14a3581648&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Senator Todd Young **[cosponsors Republican police reform bill](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=168757dbd9&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Senator Mike Braun** [b](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=70cf18259a&e=1acd9bb3d6)[elieves it's time we come together](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=14c5fc4d6d&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congresswoman Jackie Walorski **[v](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=406be866ce&e=1acd9bb3d6)[isits Worldcell Extrusions in Elkhart](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=08ddffb1bd&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Jim Banks**[appears on Fox Business](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=66965cada3&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Jim Baird**[favors Paycheck Protection Program](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e85e731f87&e=1acd9bb3d6)[ for small businesses](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=852fe9181f&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congresswoman Susan Brooks**[learns how Conner Prairie is opening safely](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a2737595ee&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Greg Pence **[introduces legislation to improve safety of Hoosier rail crossings](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=bf61ef6ea2&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Larry Bucshon**[creates virtual job board](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9e13f56f8f&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Trey Hollingsworth **[t](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b14c2dd854&e=1acd9bb3d6)[hanks frontline workers for their leadership](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=066b48c78a&e=1acd9bb3d6)

subject: Tom Cotton BEAT the New York Times

body:
The New York Times finally met its match: Senator Tom Cotton. Senator Cotton wrote an op-ed explaining his position that is supported by 58% of Americans. He argued that if cities and local law enforcement were overwhelmed by riots and looting, the military should be brought in to stop the violence.Click here to unsubscribe http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/servlet/OneClickOptOutServlet?m=42771291&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc4MTcwNzUwNQS2&mt=1&rt=0
Peter,The New York Times finally met its match: Senator Tom Cotton.

Senator Cotton wrote an op-ed explaining his position that is supported by 58% of Americans. He argued that if cities and local law enforcement were overwhelmed by riots and looting, the military should be brought in to stop the violence.

This view was immediately deemed offensive and controversial by liberal reporters and the left-wing mob.

Reporters staged a virtual walkout, and, in the end, the editorial page editor was fired for publishing an op-ed written by the Senator from Arkansas.

Looking back on some previous New York Times’ op-eds, including ones penned by the foreign minister of Iran and Vladimir Putin, I must have missed the outrage and protests that ensued.

Free speech and a healthy public discourse have made our country great. Competing ideas and viewpoints have long been debated on opinion pages of newspapers.

The fallout from Senator Cotton’s op-ed was shocking.

They lied about the origins of the op-ed and tried to smear him and claim his argument was too extreme.

Remember, this view is held by the majority of Americans.

We are witnessing a dangerous precedent develop. If you don’t agree with an opinion, you protest and demand it be taken down.

The far-left has met its match in Senator Tom Cotton. These radicals will undoubtedly do everything they can to take him down. He is a fighter for our conservative values, and we need his voice back in the Senate. http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI3NzEyOTES1&kn=17&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=2&j=MTc4MTcwNzUwNQS2&mt=1&rt=0

name: Gina Raimondo
subject: Phase 3 guidance and plans for schools
body:
Your RI COVID-19 News Update
---
| | | | | |
---
| Hello friend,
I have a lot of news to share with you today, but first I want to wish all Rhode Islanders a happy Juneteenth! Today we commemorate the moment that Union soldiers landed in Galveston, Texas--nearly three years after the Emancipation Proclamation was signed--and finally delivered the news to slaves that they had been freed. Juneteenth is a celebratory holiday, but it's also a time to reflect. Now more than ever, it's important that we recognize our history and commit ourselves to a better, more just future.
As we continue in our reopening -- and with our plan to rebuild an even stronger, more resilient, more equitable Rhode Island - we're going to have to commit to keeping our focus on public health guidelines. We'll have to hold ourselves accountable to make good choices. We're not going to get through this by relying on heavy-handed enforcement. We're going to get through this by taking pride in doing our part to protect Rhode Island.
Today, I want to tell you about some of the rules we're planning to change when we enter Phase 3.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wVZPN03XTKZkPv_aEuhVpvn7rs0zhkM2AClebC87oWD7ksK6njPQ6dSq215Czj1KgTJqKRuue-kE1nKYyTxS8fgTWYvfW40nCNcplKMLAZnij8oib0_OGMVpiX41Q8b_todfoa8YQ4KdevVWzm5J0uZYoG5vWByHsBAOZ69CWQDVsRKe7l2qQNUxi_r_56U7gpUwBw_b4SQ=&c=8WUJqI9HORVqmyEHFBSpS5dY_lblO0I_xEsi4jbvKtmVAVLbLAK7bw==&ch=ikDk_XiOvkKf9lwthXL9Z4FsEqxVsXO1ItJVC_OU1pNEg4MNiQDA7w==)
---
| In Phase 3, indoor social gatherings should be no more than 50 to 75 people. Outdoor social gatherings should be no more than 75 to 150 people. Please understand that these ranges still represent a wide spread of risk; you should try to hold your event to the lower limit in order to keep yourself and your guests safe.
Social gatherings include all parties, weddings and networking events. These are situations where you're likely going to mingle and come in contact with many people beyond the group you arrived with. As you can see, we're going to have some choices to make. There's a range here, and we just have to understand that the smaller the group we're in, the lower the risk of spreading the virus.
For a few months now, we've given very specific direction sector by sector as we've taken into account the specific circumstances of different industries and activities. As we move forward, the rules for Phase 3 are going to simplify. In Phase 3:

* All indoor settings operating at a square footage capacity can increase up to 1 person per 100 square feet provided that everyone maintains 6-feet of social distance. This includes things like retail stores and gyms.
* All indoor settings currently operating at a percent capacity cap can increase up to 66% capacity provided that everyone maintains 6-feet of social distance. This includes things like offices, restaurants and places of worship.
* Nearly all indoor venues that were closed in Phase 2 can open. This includes things like movie theaters, bowling alleys, arcades, museums and performance venues. We'll allow these venues to open at up to 66% capacity or 100 square feet per person provided that everyone maintains 6-feet of social distance.
* There will be no cap on the number of people who can be in an outdoor setting at one time. However, we do not recommend going above 250 people.

We aren't quite ready to move to Phase 3 just yet, but I hope this preview of the changes to the rules is an encouraging sign for you. I also want to tell you about what our plans are for opening schools safely.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wVZPN03XTKZkPv_aEuhVpvn7rs0zhkM2AClebC87oWD7ksK6njPQ6XezF3XNzXGd9i49soLIIf7AbT5NI8Mm73O6Pkl7hhxpw-MVwjLScD3Rrq9-hNnsx8Id7Hp43poi_upOPY8XvpDIiYVDku6322aAOc2PzTHa8xMIgz9ouQxk6QG68x5urN8NbF9Gt0FqjNi6tos9Wmc=&c=8WUJqI9HORVqmyEHFBSpS5dY_lblO0I_xEsi4jbvKtmVAVLbLAK7bw==&ch=ikDk_XiOvkKf9lwthXL9Z4FsEqxVsXO1ItJVC_OU1pNEg4MNiQDA7w==)
---
| Click the graph to see check the Department of Health's [COVID-19 data dashboard](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wVZPN03XTKZkPv_aEuhVpvn7rs0zhkM2AClebC87oWD7ksK6njPQ6XezF3XNzXGd9i49soLIIf7AbT5NI8Mm73O6Pkl7hhxpw-MVwjLScD3Rrq9-hNnsx8Id7Hp43poi_upOPY8XvpDIiYVDku6322aAOc2PzTHa8xMIgz9ouQxk6QG68x5urN8NbF9Gt0FqjNi6tos9Wmc=&c=8WUJqI9HORVqmyEHFBSpS5dY_lblO0I_xEsi4jbvKtmVAVLbLAK7bw==&ch=ikDk_XiOvkKf9lwthXL9Z4FsEqxVsXO1ItJVC_OU1pNEg4MNiQDA7w==).
---
| In order to put the health and safety of our children first, we're asking schools to prepare for three reopening scenarios that will range from limited in-person learning to full in-person learning. Each option will have two different sets of requirements--one for elementary and middle schools and one for high schools.
Full in-person learning, which is what we're aiming for on August 31, is going to require elementary and middle schools to keep kids in stable groups of 30 or fewer. That means the same 30 kids should be interacting every day--students won't be able to go from group to group. While we do not be expect that students maintain 6 feet distance at all times within these stable groups, we will always encourage as much physical distance as possible, even within these groups. And any time more than one stable group is in the same space, they will need to keep 14 feet away from each other.
For our high schools, we're recommending that students stay in stable groups whenever possible, but we recognize that this will be harder to accomplish. In high schools, students and teachers will need to either keep 6 feet of distance or wear cloth face masks whenever they cannot maintain stable groups.
If we start to see concerning trends and need to scale back, we'll move to our second scenario: partial in-person learning. For elementary and middle schools, this will look similar to full in-person learning. For high schools, we'll have to scale back and will only allow 50% of the student body to be in the building at any one time unless stable groups can be maintained at all times.
If we need to restrict in-person learning even more, we'll transition to limited-in person learning. This is where we'll prioritize the populations that benefit the most from in-person learning, like our students with differing abilities and multilingual learners. Group sizes and capacity limits will all go down.
I want to be clear that the strides we've made this year with distance learning were not for nothing. Distance learning is part of our future. We know that even in our "full in-person learning" scenario, there will be vulnerable students who will need to continue distance learning. There will be students who need to quarantine. There will be students with the sniffles who need to stay home. All students deserve the highest quality education we can provide for every one of the 180 days of the school year, regardless whether those days take place in a classroom or at home. That is our commitment to every student.
We're all in this together.
-Gina Raimondo Governor

clean_body: Your RI COVID-19 News Update | | | | | | | Hello friend, I have a lot of news to share with you today, but first I want to wish all Rhode Islanders a happy Juneteenth! Today we commemorate the moment that Union soldiers landed in Galveston, Texas--nearly three years after the Emancipation Proclamation was signed--and finally delivered the news to slaves that they had been freed. Juneteenth is a celebratory holiday, but it's also a time to reflect. Now more than ever, it's important that we recognize our history and commit ourselves to a better, more just future. As we continue in our reopening -- and with our plan to rebuild an even stronger, more resilient, more equitable Rhode Island - we're going to have to commit to keeping our focus on public health guidelines. We'll have to hold ourselves accountable to make good choices. We're not going to get through this by relying on heavy-handed enforcement. We're going to get through this by taking pride in doing our part to protect Rhode Island. Today, I want to tell you about some of the rules we're planning to change when we enter Phase 3. | == | In Phase 3, indoor social gatherings should be no more than 50 to 75 people. Outdoor social gatherings should be no more than 75 to 150 people. Please understand that these ranges still represent a wide spread of risk; you should try to hold your event to the lower limit in order to keep yourself and your guests safe. Social gatherings include all parties, weddings and networking events. These are situations where you're likely going to mingle and come in contact with many people beyond the group you arrived with. As you can see, we're going to have some choices to make. There's a range here, and we just have to understand that the smaller the group we're in, the lower the risk of spreading the virus. For a few months now, we've given very specific direction sector by sector as we've taken into account the specific circumstances of different industries and activities. As we move forward, the rules for Phase 3 are going to simplify. In Phase 3: All indoor settings operating at a square footage capacity can increase up to 1 person per 100 square feet provided that everyone maintains 6-feet of social distance. This includes things like retail stores and gyms. All indoor settings currently operating at a percent capacity cap can increase up to 66% capacity provided that everyone maintains 6-feet of social distance. This includes things like offices, restaurants and places of worship. Nearly all indoor venues that were closed in Phase 2 can open. This includes things like movie theaters, bowling alleys, arcades, museums and performance venues. We'll allow these venues to open at up to 66% capacity or 100 square feet per person provided that everyone maintains 6-feet of social distance. There will be no cap on the number of people who can be in an outdoor setting at one time. However, we do not recommend going above 250 people. We aren't quite ready to move to Phase 3 just yet, but I hope this preview of the changes to the rules is an encouraging sign for you. I also want to tell you about what our plans are for opening schools safely. | == | Click the graph to see check the Department of Health's ==. | In order to put the health and safety of our children first, we're asking schools to prepare for three reopening scenarios that will range from limited in-person learning to full in-person learning. Each option will have two different sets of requirements--one for elementary and middle schools and one for high schools. Full in-person learning, which is what we're aiming for on August 31, is going to require elementary and middle schools to keep kids in stable groups of 30 or fewer. That means the same 30 kids should be interacting every day--students won't be able to go from group to group. While we do not be expect that students maintain 6 feet distance at all times within these stable groups, we will always encourage as much physical distance as possible, even within these groups. And any time more than one stable group is in the same space, they will need to keep 14 feet away from each other. For our high schools, we're recommending that students stay in stable groups whenever possible, but we recognize that this will be harder to accomplish. In high schools, students and teachers will need to either keep 6 feet of distance or wear cloth face masks whenever they cannot maintain stable groups. If we start to see concerning trends and need to scale back, we'll move to our second scenario: partial in-person learning. For elementary and middle schools, this will look similar to full in-person learning. For high schools, we'll have to scale back and will only allow 50% of the student body to be in the building at any one time unless stable groups can be maintained at all times. If we need to restrict in-person learning even more, we'll transition to limited-in person learning. This is where we'll prioritize the populations that benefit the most from in-person learning, like our students with differing abilities and multilingual learners. Group sizes and capacity limits will all go down. I want to be clear that the strides we've made this year with distance learning were not for nothing. Distance learning is part of our future. We know that even in our "full in-person learning" scenario, there will be vulnerable students who will need to continue distance learning. There will be students who need to quarantine. There will be students with the sniffles who need to stay home. All students deserve the highest quality education we can provide for every one of the 180 days of the school year, regardless whether those days take place in a classroom or at home. That is our commitment to every student. We're all in this together. -Gina Raimondo Governor P.S.: Information is our greatest weapon in the fight against COVID-19, If you or someone you know wants to sign up to receive these emails, you can use ==. | | Governor's Office | 82 Smith Street, Providence, RI 02903 | | Sent by mailto:communications@governor.ri.gov in collaboration with |

body:

Friend,
Did you see the news?
D.A. Flynn has quickly dismissed Beth Parlato's phony complaint against me. It's now clear she orchestrated this stunt to abuse our justice system solely as a political attack and to run yet another negative television attack ad.
It's shameful, but are we that surprised? Nasty Beth Parlato has built her entire campaign on negative tactics and dirty politics.
But now she has knowingly lied to voters just days before an election. Her actions are disgusting and, in my opinion, completely disqualifying. This district has seen too much deception and lies the past few years, we don't need another politician driven blindly by their self-interest. Now more than ever we need leaders with integrity. That's why I need each and every one of you to help us secure victory on June 23rd to ensure we restore trust back to this seat. I humbly ask for your vote twice on June 23rd - once for the special election and once for the primary election. Together let's give our region the leadership it deserves.

Thank you for your support.

Sincerely,
Chris Jacobs
